name: Maiden Data Center
description:
Like many content companies, Apple operates data centers on both coasts of the United States. It owns data centers in Oregon, North Carolina, California, and Nevada. The company also leases space from colocation providers, but the bulk of its data center capacity is in company-owned facilities.

Apple announced that the electricity supporting the iDataCenter in Maiden would either be supplied by renewable energy generated on-site, or  by directly purchasing clean, renewable energy generated by local and regional sources. And the company has honored its promises over the years.

The company built three solar farms, each valued at around $10 million, as well as a 10-megawatt fuel cell installation, valued at least at $4 million. One solar farm is across the street from the Maiden data center, while another solar farm is in Conover and the third one is in Claremont. The 200-acre array of photovoltaic solar panels serves as a supplement to its utility power feed from Duke Energy as well as  fuel cells from Bloom Energy that use biogas from nearby landfills to generate electricity. Apple says its generation facilities at the Maiden, N.C. facility produce enough on-site renewable energy to power the equivalent of 14,00 homes.

Apple's focus on sustainability extends to the construction methods using in building the North Carolina facility. The Apple data center in Maiden has earned Platinum, the highest level attainable under the LEED ( Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) rating system for energy efficient buildings. The company used 14 percent of recycled materials in its construction process, and diverted 93 percent of construction waste from landfills. Apple also sourced 41 percent of purchased materials within 500 miles of the Maiden site, which reduced the environmental impact from trucking materials over long distances.

name: ATL01-01 Data Center
description:
Edged ATL01-01 (also referred to as ATL01-1) is a state-of-the-art data center facility operated by Edged Energy, located at 1800 Thomas Street NW, Atlanta, Georgia 30318, on the Edged Atlanta campus.Strategically positioned just ~5 miles from downtown Atlanta, the campus spans 80+ acres and offers 168 MW of critical IT capacity across its data center buildings.

This facility holds strong strategic significance as a high-density, AI-ready site designed for energy and water efficiency, supporting generative AI, cloud, and advanced computing workloads, while also leveraging Atlanta’s connectivity, pro-business environment, and access to major utility infrastructure. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Critical Capacity: ATL01-01 delivers 24 MW critical IT capacity. 
  • Campus Scale: Part of a larger Edged Atlanta campus which provides 168 MW total and sits on 80+ acres. 
  • Design PUE: Portfolio-average PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) of 1.15. 
  • Cooling System: Advanced waterless cooling modular system. Uses a glycol heat transfer loop with rooftop air-cooled chillers; closed-loop evaporators for secondary cooling; hot aisle containment; flexible rack densities from ~70 kW (air cooling) up to 200 kW per rack with liquid cooling options. 
  • Power & Redundancy:
  • • Redundant utility feeds from Georgia Power. 
  • • Utility voltage transformation from 115 kV to 25 kV; internal delivery via busways (415/240 V) or 208/120 V. 
  • • UPS with static backup (≈ 1 MW) and N+1 redundancy; generators with N+1 redundancy and 24-hour fuel storage. 
  • Infrastructure & Build: Two-story, new-build structure; slab floor type; loading docks (two bays); office and storage space available; 24×7×365 access. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical Security: Perimeter fencing; access gates. 
  • Internal Access Controls: Biometric readers; ID badges; man-traps; interior access segregation. 
  • Monitoring and Staffing: 24/7 manned onsite security; CCTV surveillance; real-time monitoring; full-time onsite support.
  • Certifications & Compliance: Fully compliant with industry standards for sustainability and efficiency; though no publicly confirmed third-party compliance (e.g. ISO, SOC) is explicitly listed for ATL01-01 in the sources found. (If you need those, I can try to pull up any that are documented elsewhere.)

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-Neutral Facility: Multiple carriers are supported; multiple fiber providers. 
  • Diverse Entry Points: Four diverse fiber entry points into the site’s telco duct banks. 
  • Meet-Me Rooms: Two meet-me rooms available within ATL01-01. 
  • High-Speed Fiber Connectivity: The campus is served by multiple high-speed fiber networks, supporting connectivity needed for cloud providers, AI workloads, etc. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Companies deploying AI / ML / advanced computing workloads requiring high rack density and efficient cooling
  • Cloud service providers or hyperscale operators seeking sustainable infrastructure and high availability. 
  • Enterprises in industries with strong compliance, uptime, and reliability demands. 
  • Customers for whom energy efficiency, low environmental impact, and water conservation are priorities.
full_address: 1800 Thomas Street Northwest, Atlanta, GA 30318, USA

Building 1 is part of the Santa Clara I Data Center Campus, Vantage Data Centers’ flagship campus in the heart of Silicon. Strategically located in Santa Clara, California, the campus sits at the center of one of the world’s most densely interconnected technology ecosystems and is positioned less than two miles from Vantage’s second Santa Clara campus. The campus spans 21 acres and delivers 77MW of critical IT load across six multi-story data center facilities, totaling more than 335,000 square feet. Designed to support enterprise, cloud, and high-density deployments, Building 1 benefits from diverse cooling strategies, flexible redundancy configurations, and strong carrier availability in one of the most network-rich markets globally.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  1. Located on a 21-acre campus in Santa Clara, CA
  2. 77MW of critical IT capacity across six multi-story facilities
  3. 335,000+ square feet of data center space
  4. Power provided by Silicon Valley Power, the region’s leading energy provider
  5. Class A building amenities including offices, conference rooms, and break areas
  6. Located less than two miles from Vantage’s second Santa Clara campus

❄️ Cooling Infrastructure

  1. Multiple cooling solutions available, including traditional chilled water loops, outside air economization, and chilled water to the rack
  2. Highly efficient chilled water loop system utilizing minimal water for maximum cooling efficiency
  3. Entire campus utilizes gray water for more sustainable cooling operations
  4. Newer deployments support chilled water doors at the rack for high-density configurations (35kW+ per rack)
  5. Outside air economization available in multiple data modules, enabling achievable PUEs under 1.2

🔌 Power Infrastructure

  1. Powered by Silicon Valley Power
  2. Varied redundancy configurations available across the campus (grid, N, N+1, 2N)
  3. Multiple data modules capable of supporting diverse densities, including high-density-ready configurations
  4. Supports densities up to 200W/SF

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  1. Four diverse fiber paths into the campus
  2. Both dark fiber and lit fiber services available
  3. Multiple Meet-Me-Rooms (MMRs) available in every data center
  4. All major carriers available on campus

🔐 Security

  1. Onsite Security Operations Center with patrols 24x7x365
  2. Perimeter security gates and fencing
  3. CCTV surveillance from 200+ cameras across the campus
  4. Minimum of five layers of security protecting each customer’s data module
  5. Visitor management system with controlled and monitored access policies

💼 Who It Serves

  1. Hyperscale and cloud providers operating in Silicon Valley
  2. Enterprise organizations requiring direct proximity to the Bay Area tech ecosystem
  3. High-density and AI-ready deployments leveraging advanced cooling options
  4. SaaS and digital platforms requiring strong interconnection and low latency

name: Dallas (DA1-Allen) Data Center
description:
Discover our state-of-the-art data center in Allen, TX, situated in the heart of the bustling Dallas-Fort Worth metro area. With its convenient location just 20 miles north of Dallas, our facility offers easy access to both DFW International Airport and Dallas Love Field Airport.

At Centersquare Allen, we are proud to offer a cloud-enabled infrastructure, ensuring low-latency Internet connectivity and lightning-fast connections to our data centers across the United States. Rest assured that our data center provides the top-notch physical security and resilience you would expect from a world-class provider.

Our data center features customer drop-in cubicles, making it easy for our clients to work on-site. Situated in a business park, our facility offers a spacious and professional environment for your business needs. We offer a variety of options, including cages and locking cabinets, to ensure the security of your equipment.

With a standard rack size of 45U, our data center is capable of handling your equipment requirements. Additionally, our raised floor load capacity is 150 lbs. per square foot, and our subfloor load capacity is an impressive 300 lbs. per square foot.

Cross connections are available in copper, fiber, and coaxial, allowing for seamless connectivity within our data center. Our highly skilled remote hands team is available 24/7 to assist with any on-site needs you may have.

Featuring ample electrical capacity, our facility offers 200 w/sq. ft. in cages and cabinets, with UPS configuration of N+1. We have a single utility feeder and standby power configuration of N+1. Our standby power is backed by five underground fuel tanks, storing a total of 70,000 gallons of diesel.

For optimal temperature control, our data center boasts an N+1 HVAC system, ensuring reliable and efficient cooling. Our facility is equipped with five 600-ton chillers and cooling towers, along with an on-site make-up water system.

Safety is our top priority, which is why we have implemented a VESDA smoke detection system and a conventional addressable fire alarm system throughout our data center. In our data center areas, we have a pre-action dry pipe fire suppression system, providing robust protection for your valuable equipment.

Experience the excellence of our Centersquare Allen data center, where we go above and beyond to deliver top-tier facilities and services for all your data storage needs.

name: Edged Chicago Data Center
description:
ORD01-1 (“ORD01-1 Data Center, Edged Chicago”) is a 24 MW critical-capacity facility located at 2835 Bilter Road, Aurora, Illinois, operated by Edged US (an Endeavour company). It is part of the ORD01 campus, one of three planned buildings across a 65+ acre high-tech campus. The facility is positioned about 35 minutes from downtown Chicago, offering strategic proximity to major airports, universities, and commercial centers in the greater Chicagoland region. ORD01-1 is designed to support high-density, AI-capable workloads, with modern infrastructure optimized for both performance and sustainability. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Critical capacity: 24 MW in the ORD01-1 building. 
  • Gross floor / whitespace: approximately 210,000 square feet of space.
  • Energy efficiency: Designed portfolio average PUE of 1.15, which is significantly better than the global industry average. 
  • Cooling systems: Advanced waterless cooling; glycol heat transfer via rooftop, air-cooled chillers; closed-loop evaporators; flexible hot-aisle containment; supports up to 70 kW/rack (air) and 200 kW/rack (liquid cooling integration). 
  • Redundancy & reliability: Tier III design (concurrently maintainable), N+1 redundancy in UPS, dual power source, 2N piping. 
  • Backup: 1 MW static UPS with about 9.3 minutes backup, N+1 generator backup, 24-hour fuel supply. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical security: Exterior perimeter fencing, access gates; interior biometric readers; ID badge access; CCTV surveillance; 24/7 on-site (manned) security staff. 
  • Access control & monitoring: Biometric plus badge control; continuous monitoring via secure DCIM platform for mechanical and power systems. 
  • SLA & uptime: 100 % uptime SLA was claimed for ORD01-1. 
  • Environmental and sustainability certifications: While not every compliance certification (for data security as SOC, ISO etc.) is explicitly listed for ORD01-1, the facility emphasizes sustainable design (waterless systems, reduced emissions). 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with multiple fiber providers. 
  • Telco infrastructure: Four diverse entry points (duct banks) for telco/fiber entry. 
  • Two meet-me rooms available to facilitate interconnection among carriers and tenants. 
  • Long-haul / metro network access: The facility lies on a campus with “world-class fiber infrastructure” and connections to multiple long-haul networks across the U.S. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises running AI / high-performance computing or workloads requiring high rack density and advanced cooling capabilities. 
  • Companies prioritizing sustainability and low environmental impact (e.g. low water usage, reduced emissions). 
  • Organizations requiring high reliability, uptime and redundancy—for critical infrastructure, financial services, cloud providers, etc.
  • Customers who need robust connectivity options, multiple carriers, and flexible interconnect / meet-me capabilities.

name: Edged Kansas City Data Center
description:
Edged Kansas City is a cutting-edge, sustainable data center located at 3420 North Arlington Avenue, Kansas City, Missouri, operated by Edged Data Centers (an Endeavour company).

The facility occupies a 50+ acre campus, with a 124,645 sq ft building footprint (new-build, one story, pre-engineered metal building) designed to support high-density and AI-driven workloads. 

 It delivers approximately 24-26 megawatts of critical capacity. 

Strategically positioned just north of Highway 210 and east of I-435, the site is less than 20 minutes from downtown Kansas City, offering proximity to major fiber networks and transcontinental cables.

This data center is distinguished by its sustainable design—including waterless cooling, ultra-efficient energy usage, and infrastructure ready for high rack densities—making it a regional gateway for hyperscale, AI, and enterprise clients. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Critical Power Capacity: ~26 MW (recent expansions) of critical infrastructure power. 
  • Energy Efficiency (PUE): Portfolio-average design PUE of 1.15. 
  • Cooling System: ThermalWorks modular waterless cooling system that consumes zero water for cooling; supports up to ~70 kW/rack with air cooling and up to ~200 kW/rack when using plug-and-play liquid cooling. 
  • Zero Water Usage: Designed to use no water for cooling, saving nearly 95 million gallons of water annually compared to traditional data centers. 
  • Sustainability Investment: The facility represents a ~$143 million initial investment. 
  • Site / Building Details: 124,645 sq ft building on over 50 acres site; one-story metal building; slab floor; two loading dock bays; 24×7×365 access. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Reliability / SLA: 100% uptime SLA. Tier III design with concurrently maintainable infrastructure. 
  • Redundancy: A/B side distribution; UPS systems with N+1 redundancy; generator backup with N+1 redundancy. 
  • Physical Security & Monitoring: The facility has 24×7×365 access control; multiple fiber providers with diverse fiber entry points; interior biometric readers, ID badges, CCTV; perimeter fencing and gated access; meet-me rooms. 
  • Environmental & Emissions Controls: Standby generation systems use Tier 4 Final engines (greatly reduced NOx and particulate emissions); water usage effectiveness (WUE) targeting zero liters/kWh of cooling; uses low/global warming potential refrigerants (e.g. R1234ze or R515-B) in cooling systems. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Fiber & Network Proximity: Near major fiber networks and transcontinental cable routes; multiple fiber providers; four diverse points of fiber entry into the facility. 
  • Carrier Neutrality: The facility has multiple fiber providers, implying carrier neutrality; includes meet-me rooms to support interconnection demands. 
  • Interconnection Services & Support for High Demand Workloads: Designed to serve hyperscale, cloud, AI, and enterprise customers who need high density racks, low latency, and robust connectivity. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Hyperscale cloud providers needing large scale, efficient capacity. 
  • Companies with AI/ML workloads or high performance computing needing very high rack densities. 
  • Enterprises seeking highly sustainable, next-gen infrastructure (low energy overhead, minimal environmental impact).
  • Organizations that require strong connectivity, low latency, and robust networking across U.S. markets.

name: Secaucus (NYC3) Data Center
description:
Centersquare Secaucus: The Ideal Data Center Location for Manhattan

Located just on the other side of the Lincoln Tunnel and only 14 miles from Newark Airport, Centersquare Secaucus is perfectly situated to serve the needs of Manhattan businesses. Not only does it provide convenient access to public clouds and high-speed connectivity to Centersquare's other US data centers, but it also offers low-latency Internet connectivity.

But that's not all. Centersquare Secaucus is committed to sustainability, boasting a carbon footprint-reducing 750kW of solar power generated onsite. Additionally, with a historical uptime of 99.999%, you can trust that your data will always be accessible and secure.

The facility itself is impressive, with a total size of 154,691 sq. ft. and a raised floor area of 98,600 sq. ft. It features cage and locking cabinet options, as well as standard racks that can support up to 44U. The raised floor has a load capacity of 150 lbs. per sq. ft., and the sub floor can handle up to 300 lbs. per sq. ft.

For convenience and peace of mind, Centersquare Secaucus offers 24x7 remote hands support and has a loading dock for easy equipment transportation. On the electrical side, it provides an impressive capacity of 200 watts per sq. ft., with various cabinet options. The UPS configuration is 2N, and there are two utility feeders for redundancy. In case of power outages, the data center has ten 2,000 kW diesel generators and five 10,000-gallon above-ground fuel tanks for standby power.

When it comes to cooling, Centersquare Secaucus has it covered. With an N+1 HVAC system and distribution, as well as five 600-ton chillers/cooling towers and an on-site 40,000-gallon make-up water supply, your equipment will stay cool and protected.

Safety is a top priority as well. The data center features VESDA smoke detection and a conventional addressable fire alarm system. Fire suppression systems are zoned, with pre-action dry pipe systems in the data center areas and wet sprinklers in non-equipment areas.

In summary, Centersquare Secaucus offers the perfect combination of location, reliability, sustainability, and top-notch infrastructure to meet your data center needs. Trust us with your data, and experience the difference for yourself.

description:
The Vantage Frontier Campus, developed by Vantage Data Centers, is a mega‑scale data center project located in Shackelford County, Texas, approximately 120 miles west of Dallas–Fort Worth and near Abilene. This expansive campus—spanning 1,200 acres—is one of the largest AI‑optimized data center developments globally, designed to serve the surging demand for hyperscale GPU compute infrastructure.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Massive Scale: Comprises 10 single-story data center buildings, totaling 3.7 million sq ft of gross floor space.
  • Power Capacity: Designed to deliver 1.4 gigawatts (GW) of critical IT load across the campus.
  • Ultra-High Rack Density: Capable of supporting rack power densities exceeding 250 kW+, ideal for AI and GPU-centric workloads.
  • Cooling & Environmental Systems:
  • Employs liquid-to-liquid cooling systems to manage high thermal densities.
  • Utilizes closed-loop chillers, dramatically reducing water usage and aiming for near-zero WUE (Water Usage Effectiveness).
  • Designed with N+1 redundancy across electrical and cooling infrastructure.
  • Sustainability: Pursuing LEED certification, with a “sustainable‑by‑design” blueprint emphasizing energy and water efficiency.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Resilient Design: Built with N+1 redundancy across backup generators, UPS, and cooling units for operational reliability.
  • Operational Excellence: Staffed by in‑house operations teams and adhering to uniform global standards for service and delivery across all Vantage sites.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Robust Network Infrastructure: Designed to include multiple, diverse fiber pathways with access to both lit and dark fiber for redundancy. Likely features meet‑me rooms and multiple Points of Entry per building, supporting extensive interconnection.

💼 Who It Serves

This campus targets hyperscale AI cloud providers, content delivery networks, enterprise AI users, and operators of GPU-intensive workloads. Its scale and capabilities position it as a cornerstone for next-gen compute infrastructure in North America.

name: Ashburn Data Center Campus - Building 1
description:
Ashburn I Data Center Campus – Building 1 is part of Vantage Data Centers’ flagship Northern Virginia campus, located in Ashburn — widely recognized as the world’s data center capital. The 52-acre campus is designed to support hyperscale, enterprise, and cloud deployments at scale, offering access to one of the most network-dense ecosystems globally. With robust utility infrastructure, advanced cooling architecture, and extensive carrier access, Building 1 benefits from the broader campus design built for high-density, mission-critical workloads.

Strategically positioned in Loudoun County, the campus offers proximity to major network routes, enterprise hubs, and Washington, D.C. metro infrastructure. The Ashburn I campus delivers a total of 206MW of IT capacity across six data centers, spanning approximately 1.7 million square feet, supporting scalable and performance-driven digital infrastructure.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  1. Located within a 52-acre hyperscale campus in Ashburn, Virginia
  2. Part of a campus delivering 206MW of critical IT load capacity
  3. Up to 300W per square foot average density, supporting high-performance workloads
  4. 480V end-to-end electrical distribution
  5. Multiple, diverse power feeds serving the campus
  6. Power provided by Dominion Energy, the leading regional utility provider
  7. Designed for scalable deployments suitable for enterprise, cloud, and hyperscale customers

❄️ Cooling Architecture

  1. Computer Room Air Handling (CRAH) units located in dual galleries on opposite sides of each data module for optimized airflow distribution
  2. Closed-loop chilled water system with air-side economizers
  3. N+2 redundancy across all mechanical systems
  4. Water Utilization Efficiency (WUE) near zero (liters/kW/hr) using advanced cooling design

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  1. All major fiber-based service providers operating within two miles of the campus
  2. Multiple Meet-Me-Rooms (MMRs) throughout the campus enabling diverse connectivity paths
  3. Four campus Points-of-Entry (POEs) ensuring maximum inbound carrier diversity
  4. Diverse fiber pathways into the campus (minimum two paths per carrier)

Ashburn I provides direct access to the largest peering and cloud ecosystem in North America, making Building 1 well suited for low-latency, network-intensive, and hybrid cloud architectures.

🔐 Security

  1. 24x7x365 on-site Security Operations Center with patrols
  2. Perimeter fencing and security gates
  3. CCTV coverage across all access control points throughout the campus
  4. Dual authentication (badge/PIN plus biometric readers) for customer and critical infrastructure areas
  5. Visitor management system with controlled badging and personnel tracking

🏢 Amenities

  1. Class A building amenities including office space, conference rooms, and break rooms
  2. Customizable office and workspace areas dedicated to individual customers
  3. Secure storage with convenient access between data modules
  4. Multiple meeting and collaboration spaces throughout the campus
  5. Proximity to Reston Town Center, One Loudoun, and Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD) VDC_DataSheet_Virginia-I_EN-1

💼 Who It Serves

  1. Hyperscale cloud providers deploying large-scale regional capacity
  2. Enterprises requiring access to Northern Virginia’s dense carrier ecosystem
  3. SaaS and platform providers serving East Coast and global markets
  4. Financial services, government contractors, and regulated industries
  5. Organizations implementing hybrid IT and multi-site architectures
full_address: Vantage Data Centers, Sterling, VA 20166, USA

name: Edged Columbus Data Center
description:
Edged Columbus, operated by Edged (a subsidiary of Endeavour), is a state-of-the-art data center located at 6525 New Albany Road East, New Albany, Ohio, less than 20 minutes from downtown Columbus in the New Albany Business Park. 

Positioned in one of the U.S.’s fastest-growing cloud and AI hubs—often dubbed the “Silicon Heartland”—this facility addresses rising demand for high-performance computing, AI training/inference, and sustainable infrastructure. Its strategic location offers access to robust utility infrastructure, favorable land and regulatory conditions, and strong fiber connectivity across both East Coast and Midwest markets. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Critical Capacity: 24 MW of critical load.
  • Size & Campus: Building footprint is ~206,000 sq ft on a 14+-acre site. Two-story new-build construction with dual loading dock bays. 
  • Cooling & Efficiency:
  • Advanced waterless cooling via ThermalWorks modular system. 
  • Supports up to ~70 kW per rack for air cooling, and ~200 kW per rack with plug-and-play liquid cooling integration. 
  • Portfolio-wide design PUE of ~1.15, significantly better than industry averages. 
  • Sustainability / Water Usage:
  • Expected to save ~94.8-95 million gallons of water annually compared to conventional data centers. 
  • Water Usage Effectiveness (WUE) zero liters/kWh of cooling usage. 
  • Reliability & Redundancy:
  • Designed for concurrent maintenance with Tier III design principles. 
  • UPS system with N+1 redundancy; standby generators with 24-hour fuel supply; dual power feeds. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24×7×365 access with onsite access controls. 
  • Dual meet-me rooms. 
  • Addressable smoke detectors; hot aisle early detection; dual interlock pre-action sprinkler systems in data halls. 
  • Battery backup supporting all electrical and mechanical systems.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Multiple fiber providers with four diverse points of entry. 
  • Multiple meet-me rooms, enabling interconnection / cross-connects. 
  • Strong connectivity to both East-Coast and Midwest markets, leveraging regional fiber networks.

💼 Who It Serves

  • Organizations operating AI training & inference workloads and high-density compute. 
  • Hyperscale cloud providers and large enterprises needing high reliability and sustainability. 
  • Industries with strict sustainability goals or regulatory pressure on water usage and carbon / energy efficiency. 
  • Companies requiring low‐latency connectivity across the U.S. Midwest / East markets.
full_address: 6525 New Albany Road East, New Albany, OH, USA

name: Boston (BO1-Watertown) Data Center
description:
Welcome to Centersquare's cutting-edge Watertown data center, conveniently located near the heart of Boston and just 10 miles from Boston Logan International Airport. With a strong focus on reliability and accessibility, our state-of-the-art facility boasts resilient power and cooling systems, ensuring an impressive "five nines" uptime reliability. Additionally, we offer ample expansion space for customer needs, seamless access to public clouds, high-speed connectivity to our other US data centers, and low-latency Internet connections.

Our data center spans an impressive 201,590 sq. ft., with a raised floor area of 62,257 sq. ft. We provide various types of storage options, including cages and locking cabinets. Our standard cabinets are 45U with a depth of 42", and can support 4 & 6 kW.

We prioritize the safety and security of your data with our robust infrastructure. The floor load capacity is 150 lbs. / sq. ft., and the subfloor load capacity is 250 lbs. / sq. ft. We offer flexible cross connect options, including copper, fiber, and coaxial.

At Centersquare, we understand the importance of a comfortable and efficient working environment. That's why we provide office space with customer drop-in cubicles. Our facility also includes 24x7 remote hands support and a loading dock for easy logistics.

When it comes to electrical capacity, we offer top-of-the-line solutions. For cages, we can deliver up to 300 w/sq. ft., while cabinets (2.4 & 4.3 kW) have their own electrical capacity. Our UPS configuration is 2N for added redundancy, and we have three utility feeders for reliable power supply. With an N+1 standby power configuration, we have eight 2,000 kW diesel generators and four 20,000 gallon underground fuel tanks.

When it comes to cooling, our N+1 HVAC system ensures optimal temperature regulation. We have N+1 chillers and three 16,000 gallon onsite make-up water tanks for efficient HVAC distribution.

Choose Centersquare's Watertown data center for a state-of-the-art facility that prioritizes reliability, accessibility, and optimal performance for all your data storage needs.
full_address: 486 Arsenal Way, Watertown, MA, USA

name: ORD1 Chicago Data Center Campus

The building was originally constructed for R.R. Donnelley & Sons Printing in 1912, and the Sears Catalog was printed here until 1993. While it was renovated for data center usage in 1999, the building is historically landmarked and protected. The building is 9 minutes from downtown Chicago, 25 minutes from Midway International Airport (MDW) and 30 minutes from O’Hare International Airport (ORD).

Highlights

  • 183,419 sq/ft and 8.5 MW of power, located outside the 100-year flood zone
  • Fiber-linked campus between the 2 data centers in the heart of Chicago’s leading financial and telecom exchange

  • Customer community of over 330 financial services firms, XaaS providers, media companies, and other large enterprises
  • Compliance with SSAE18, SOC-1, SOC-2, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, and NIST 800-53PE standards
  • HIPAA aligned
  • Connectivity to 22 unique network service providers and an average of 16 providers per Chicago data center
  • Solution deployment options include individual Secure Cabinets and dedicated Secure Cages

Amenities

  • Food and beverage vending machines
  • Crash carts and loaner tools
  • Work kiosks
  • Conference room

Technical Specifications

Utility Power 8.5 MW

UPS Capacity 4.5 MW

UPS Configuration N+1

Generator Capacity 20 MW

Generator Configuration N+1

Fuel Capacity 25,000 Gal.

Cooling Configuration N+1

Cooling Capacity 1,950 Tons

Carriers

AT&T, Cablevision, CenturyLink, Cogent, Console, Hibernia, Internap, Level 3, Lexent, Lightower, Megaport, NexGen Networks, Packetfabric, Perseus Telecom, Sidera, Sprint, Verizon, XO, Zayo

name: 3199 Pilot Knob Rd
description:
3199 Pilot Knob Road in Eagan, Minnesota, is a notable commercial property that has served as a significant data center and office space. Built in 1986, this two-story building encompasses approximately 248,480 square feet, including 118,000 square feet of raised floor space designed for data center operations.

Key Features:

  • Data Center Capabilities: The facility has been utilized for extensive data center operations, offering substantial raised floor space suitable for various IT infrastructure needs.
  • Strategic Location: Situated in Eagan, a suburb of the Twin Cities, the property benefits from proximity to major transportation routes and a robust technological infrastructure, making it an attractive site for data center operations.​

Recent Developments:

In September 2023, Unisys Corporation announced plans to sell and vacate the 32-acre campus at 3199 Pilot Knob Road. The company intended to relocate its associates, data center, and lab space to another location within the Minneapolis market, though a specific relocation site had not been finalized at that time. The property was listed for sale with Cushman & Wakefield, and its valuation was estimated at approximately $13.2 million. ​

This development is part of a broader trend in the area, with other significant properties undergoing transitions. For instance, Thompson Reuters announced plans to sell a substantial portion of its Eagan campus, including data centers and office spaces. The city of Eagan has been proactive in assessing the potential reuse of these properties, considering redevelopment options that may include residential, commercial, or public spaces. ​

The future of 3199 Pilot Knob Road remains a subject of interest, as its strategic location and existing infrastructure offer potential for various redevelopment opportunities in the evolving landscape of Eagan's commercial real estate market.
full_address: 3199 Pilot Knob Road, Eagan, MN, USA

name: Quincy Data Center Campus - Building 1
description:
Building 1 is part of the Quincy Data Center Campus, a large-scale Vantage development in one of the lowest power-cost regions in the United States. Located in Quincy, Washington, the campus offers an alternative to higher-cost urban data center markets, leveraging abundant hydropower and state tax incentives to deliver a low total cost of ownership (TCO) environment.

The campus spans 68 acres and provides approximately 775,000 square feet of total leasable data center space, with 89MW of critical IT load potential. Building 1 benefits from the campus’ scalable design, incorporating redundant mechanical systems, efficient cooling architecture, and diverse connectivity options. Designed for enterprise and hyperscale deployments, the campus supports power densities up to 200W per square foot, accommodating a range of compute and cloud workloads.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  1. Located on a 68-acre campus in Quincy, WA
  2. Approximately 775,000 SF of total leasable data center space
  3. 89MW of critical IT load potential
  4. Hydropower-driven market offering among the lowest TCO in the U.S.
  5. Washington state tax incentives further reducing operational costs
  6. Customizable offices and workspaces dedicated to individual customers
  7. Secure storage and multiple conference rooms across the campus

❄️ Cooling Infrastructure

  1. Computer Room Air Handling (CRAH) units located in dual galleries on opposite sides of each data module for efficient airflow distribution
  2. Closed-loop chilled water system with air-side economizers
  3. N+1 redundancy across all mechanical systems
  4. Planned Water Utilization Efficiency (WUE) near zero using the latest cooling design

🔌 Power Infrastructure

  1. Powered by Grant County Power Utility District, offering among the lowest per-kW rates in the country
  2. Multiple, diverse power feeds supporting the campus
  3. Planned on-site substation
  4. 600V electrical design at the PDU to maximize electrical efficiency

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  1. Diverse fiber paths into the campus with additional routes planned
  2. Multiple Meet-Me-Rooms (MMRs) in each building
  3. Multiple lit carriers available, including Frontier Communications, CenturyLink, NoaNet, Noel Communications, and StarTouch
  4. Dark fiber options available from providers such as Zayo, SDN Next Generation Network Platform, and PacketFabric (location-dependent)

🔐 Security

  1. Security personnel onsite 24/7
  2. Minimum of five layers of security protecting each customer data module
  3. CCTV surveillance across the campus with 30-day video archiving
  4. Visitor management systems and badging to track and control all personnel onsite

💼 Who It Serves

  1. Hyperscale and cloud providers seeking low-cost U.S. capacity
  2. Enterprise organizations focused on long-term TCO optimization
  3. AI and compute deployments benefiting from hydropower economics
  4. SaaS and digital platform providers expanding in the Pacific Northwest
  5. Organizations requiring geographic diversity from major coastal markets

name: DFW-03 Data Center
description:
The DFW‑03 Data Center, operated by Aligned Data Centers, is located on a 27-acre campus in Mansfield (Dallas–Fort Worth Metroplex), Texas, offering strategic proximity to major business hubs, highways and the regional fibre-network corridor. 

Positioned in a low-seismic-risk zone with access to a dedicated on-site substation and abundant utility infrastructure, the site supports large-scale deployments while maintaining high resiliency and operational robustness.

The facility is designed with modular, adaptive infrastructure that supports both traditional enterprise workloads and high-density cloud/AI computing, with cooling capabilities built for high rack-densities (350 kW+/rack). 

With energy-efficint engineering and sustainability-driven design, the site aligns with Aligned’s approach to minimize water/energy use and accelerate deployments through prefabrication.

Ideal for hyperscale, enterprise and cloud deployments, the DFW-03 campus offers scale, flexibility and connectivity in one of the U.S.’s fastest-growing data-centre markets.

⚙️ Facility Highlights —

  • The campus supports future expansion of 5,000 + MW of capacity across the Aligned network, with this site built to support high-density compute workloads including GPU and AI deployments.
  • Employs a hybrid cooling architecture—air cooling and liquid cooling (or hybrid) within the same data hall—enabling support of densities in excess of 350 kW per rack.
  • Built with an adaptive modular design using prefabricated components for fast deployment, reducing cost and risk while enabling rapid scale-up. i
  • Resilient infrastructure includes on-site substation, robust primary power and cooling redundancy planned for enterprise-grade SLAs; the design emphasises sustainability with advanced PUE performance and modular growth. 

🔐 Security & Compliance —

  • The facility will operate with 24×7 staffed security, comprehensive CCTV coverage, biometric and man-trap access systems, plus early-warning fire detection systems (VESDA or equivalent) and full fire-zone separation architecture. (Aligned’s standards applied network-wide).
  • Certifications and compliance frameworks are in place to support enterprise workloads: e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type 2, PCI-DSS; the site is also HIPAA-compliant to support sensitive workloads.
  • Built to deliver high-availability SLAs, the DFW-03 site is engineered for mission-critical operations, offering enterprise customers reliable performance, resiliency and full operational transparency.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access —

  • Located in the heart of the DFW metro area, the site offers exceptional access to local, regional and international network ecosystems and major fibre routes. 
  • As a carrier-neutral facility, it provides direct connectivity via cross-connects, dark- and lit-fibre options, cloud-on-ramps to major public cloud providers and peering opportunities for content and network providers. 
  • With its Dallas–Fort Worth location, the site benefits from low latency to major hubs across the U.S., diverse network paths and the ability to interconnect with other campuses and network clusters in the region for redundancy and performance.

💼 Who It Serves —

  • Hyperscale cloud and AI/ML providers seeking high-density, scalable infrastructure for GPU, HPC and large-scale compute-intensive workloads.
  • Enterprises and SaaS companies migrating to modern digital operations, requiring flexible colocation or build-to-scale infrastructure with enterprise credentials.
  • Telecom and network operators looking to leverage central U.S. geography, strong connectivity and carrier-neutral access for regional distribution.
  • Media and content platforms streaming or distributing high-volume data, requiring low-latency interconnects and software delivery networks.
  • Organizations scaling digital infrastructure in the region (e-commerce, FinTech, IoT) that need a combination of rapid deployment, flexibility, and enterprise-grade facilities.

name: Miami Data Center
description:
The QuadraNet Miami Data Center, located at 36 Northeast 2nd Street, Miami, Florida, serves as a critical gateway between North America, Latin America, and the Caribbean. This facility sits within a highly connected interconnection building (leased space in the Digital Realty building at that address) and benefits from its role in Miami’s dense fiber and carrier ecosystem. As a colocation anchor in Miami, QuadraNet positions this facility to support high-performance content delivery, cloud, enterprise, and network operator workloads with resilience, low latency, and strong connectivity. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Floor area: 162,140 sq ft 
  • Utility / power capacity: 2.0 MW 
  • Dual power feeds (underground FPL electrical grid paths) 
  • Generator backup: two diesel generators with automatic transfer, configured for 2N redundancy, plus roll-up backup generation 
  • Cooling: advanced climate control, hot-aisle containment, efficient CRAC units 
  • Structural resilience: building is designed to withstand Category 5 hurricanes
  • On-site staff: in-house electrical engineers and electricians to deploy circuits, maintain systems 24/7 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7 manned building security and access control 
  • Biometric scanners, multi-layered access control, CCTV surveillance covering critical areas 
  • Building-level access controls, perimeter security and monitored ingress/egress 
  • While public sources do not clearly list specific certifications for this site, it's built and operated to high standards of reliability and compliance per QuadraNet’s broader operations.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with fiber path diversity 
  • Dense ecosystem: the building is a known interconnection hub (Digital Realty building with multiple carriers) 
  • Direct access to multiple Tier-1 networks and regional carriers; cross-connect availability in building fabric 
  • Redundant Points of Entry (PoE) for fiber for resiliency 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises, cloud providers, and digital businesses needing low-latency connectivity to Latin America and Caribbean
  • Content delivery networks (CDNs) and media companies distributing to the Americas
  • Telcos, network operators, and ISPs seeking interconnection and backbone provisioning in Miami
  • Businesses with emphasis on resilience, disaster preparedness (especially given hurricane risk)
  • Workloads requiring high availability colocation with robust power and backup infrastructure
full_address: 36 Northeast 2nd Street, Miami, FL, USA

name: TX22 - San Antonio Data Center
description:
The Vantage Data Centers – TX22 facility is a large-scale hyperscale data center development located in San Antonio, Texas, within Bexar County. Positioned at 5207 Rogers Road, the TX22 project is part of Vantage’s growing presence in San Antonio’s West and Far-West technology corridor, an area rapidly becoming a key hub for hyperscale, AI, and enterprise cloud deployments in the southern United States.

As part of Vantage’s continued expansion strategy, TX22 complements the nearby TX21 facility, creating a multi-facility campus capable of supporting large-scale workloads and scalable infrastructure. The project reinforces Vantage’s mission to deliver high-density, sustainable, and future-ready data centers across North America’s fastest-growing digital markets.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

The TX22 San Antonio Data Center is designed with scalability and efficiency at its core, leveraging modular construction and advanced engineering standards:

  • The facility will consist of a two-story building totaling approximately 214,525 square feet of purpose-built data center space.
  • Construction is expected to begin in October 2025, with an estimated completion date of June 2027.
  • The total project investment is projected at approximately $272 million, reflecting Vantage’s long-term commitment to the San Antonio market.
  • Located adjacent to the TX21 development, TX22 expands the regional Vantage San Antonio campus, offering combined large-scale capacity and operational synergy.
  • The design supports wholesale, hyperscale, and high-density colocation workloads, optimized for both cloud and AI-driven infrastructure.
  • Built with Tier III+ reliability, including N+1 redundancy across power and cooling systems, ensuring consistent performance and uptime.
  • Sustainability features include energy-efficient mechanical systems, low-water-use cooling, and renewable energy integration aligned with Vantage’s global environmental goals.

🔐 Security & Compliance

Vantage Data Centers maintains global security and operational excellence across all its campuses, and TX22 will meet or exceed these standards:

  • 24×7×365 on-site security staff with biometric access controls, keycard systems, and man-trap entries.
  • Perimeter fencing, vehicle barriers, and intrusion detection systems supported by continuous CCTV monitoring.
  • Redundant utility feeds, fire suppression systems, and fault-tolerant infrastructure engineered for maximum uptime.
  • Compliance aligned with ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, and PCI DSS frameworks.
  • Continuous real-time monitoring through Building Management Systems (BMS) and Data Center Infrastructure Management (DCIM) platforms.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

The TX22 facility is strategically designed for carrier neutrality and network resilience, offering exceptional connectivity for local and national workloads:

  • Multiple fiber entry points and diverse routing ensure redundancy and low-latency performance.
  • Supports dark fiber, cross-connects, and private interconnections, enabling hybrid and multi-cloud architectures.
  • Located in a rapidly expanding San Antonio corridor with direct access to major power and fiber networks.
  • Offers low-latency links to key metropolitan areas across Texas and the broader southern United States.
  • Ideal for hyperscale and enterprise customers seeking network flexibility and regional redundancy.

💼 Who It Serves

The TX22 San Antonio Data Center serves a wide range of customers and industry applications:

  • Hyperscale cloud and AI providers requiring large-scale, high-density, and sustainable infrastructure.
  • Enterprises and SaaS companies seeking secure, scalable colocation and hybrid-cloud deployments.
  • Telecommunication and content providers expanding network and edge presence in the South-Central U.S.
  • AI, HPC, and analytics-focused organizations that demand advanced compute power and energy-efficient operations.
  • Government and financial-sector clients requiring compliance-driven, secure, and reliable U.S.-based hosting environments.
full_address: 5207 Rogers Rd, San Antonio, Texas 78251, USA

name: NJ3 Carlstadt Data Center
description:
The NJ3 Carlstadt Data Center, operated by 365 Data Centers, is located at 410 Commerce Boulevard, Carlstadt, New Jersey. Situated just about ten miles west of Manhattan and 15 miles from Newark International Airport, the site offers ultra-low latency connectivity to New York City and direct access to East Coast markets. Because of its proximity to a major metropolitan hub and robust fiber infrastructure, it serves as a prime location for enterprises seeking disaster recovery, network interconnection, cloud connectivity, and secure colocation in the New York metro region. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • 187,600 sq ft of data center space (with ~4,000 sq ft of dedicated administrative area) 
  • Raisedfloor depths of 24″ and 48″, with overhead cabling and flood/fire sensors throughout the facility 
  • Hot-aisle / cold-aisle layout with cold-aisle containment in data halls
  • Total power capacity of 18 MW 
  • 2N UPS architecture with 30 UPS units (redundant) 
  • N + 1 generator backup with a 4,000-gallon fuel tank 
  • Cooling via a 4,000-ton cooling plant with N + 1 redundancy 
  • Redundant utility feeds from diverse substations 
  • Fiber diversity with multiple entry points terminating into multiple telco/IDF rooms (3 distinct telco rooms) 
  • Support for up to ~275 carrier network PoPs in the region via on-net and cross-connect options

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7/365 multi-layer physical security and perimeter monitoring 
  • 181 internal and external CCTV cameras, with video retention of 90 days
  • Controlled access (badge, escorting of visitors) and secure corridors for authorized personnel only 
  • Onsite remote-hands / technical support services available 24/7 
  • Fire suppression via FM-200 systems and Fenwal protection in raised floor areas 
  • Smoke detection systems (HSSD) and building monitoring systems integrated for early detection 
  • Compliance certifications including HIPAA, ISAE-3402, PCI DSS, SOC 2, SSAE 18 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility, enabling choice across networks and indirect peering options
  • On-site presence of multiple major networks: AT&T, Lumen (Level 3), Verizon Business (MCI/XO), Zayo, Crown Castle / Lightower, Altice (Optimum), Astound (RCN), Megaport, Cogent, Comcast, Hudson Fiber, IPC, among others
  • Diverse fiber paths and multiple telco rooms to support redundant routing and cross-connect architecture 
  • Integrated connectivity to Internet exchanges and broad regional interconnection reach via 275 carrier PoPs in the region 
  • Internal high-capacity backbone enabling cross-site and intra-facility interconnections with minimal latency 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises requiring low-latency connectivity to New York City and adjacent markets
  • Cloud service providers, SaaS vendors, and content delivery networks seeking interconnection density
  • Financial services firms needing resilient, highly secure infrastructure close to financial markets
  • Disaster recovery / business continuity customers for NYC / East Coast presence
  • Managed service providers and network operators seeking a central colocation node for aggregation

NY10, operated by 365 Data Centers, is located at 65 Broadway, New York, NY, in the heart of Manhattan’s Financial District, just one block off Wall Street. Its strategic positioning delivers ultra-low latency connectivity to financial institutions, trading firms, and mission-critical enterprise workloads.The facility also benefits from proximity to major airports (14 miles from Newark) and robust public transport access (NJ Transit, Staten Island ferry) enhancing accessibility for customers and operations teams.

NY10 serves as a key node in 365 Data Centers’ metro footprint, offering a dense carrier ecosystem and reliable infrastructure in a central business hub. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Approx. 16,000 sq ft of raised-floor colocation space with 24″ raised floor and under-floor cabling 
  • 3 × 203 kW UPS systems in an N+1 arrangement for AC loads 
  • Onsite 750 kW diesel generator for backup power during utility outages 
  • 170-ton cooling plant configured with N+1 redundancy, plus hot-aisle / cold-aisle containment
  • Support for DC power A & B feed options
  • Flood & fire sensors throughout the building, multiple monitoring systems 
  • Offers half / full cabinets, locked cabinets, cages, private suites, and modular raised floor layout

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7/365 multiple-layer physical security including restricted access, perimeter monitoring, CCTV, video surveillance 
  • Access control via security access cards + unique PINs; onsite alarm & monitoring systems
  • Building houses NYPD offices, reinforcing security posture through local government presence
  • Infrastructure and perimeter systems monitored 24/7 via Customer Support Center (CSC)
  • Remote Hands support by trained technicians available around the clock 
  • Compliance with industry standards: HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SSAE-18, SOC 2, ISAE-3402
  • Historically certified SSAE 16 / SOC I Type II

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with PoP presence for ~275 carriers across 365’s network footprint
  • Onsite connectivity from leading carriers including AT&T, Lumen (CenturyLink), Cogent, Zayo, Verizon, Crown Castle, NTT, Charter, Astound, among others 
  • Cross-connect capabilities to fiber, Ethernet, coax, POTS options 
  • Listed in PeeringDB, enabling peering relationships and exchange connectivity with 10 networks locally 
  • Redundant fiber entry diversity and resilient routing architecture (as per 365’s metro interconnect fabric) 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Financial services / trading firms needing ultra-low latency infrastructure in Manhattan’s core
  • Enterprises requiring secure colocation, disaster recovery, or business continuity near New York City
  • Telecommunications & network operators seeking robust interconnection and peering options
  • Cloud, SaaS, fintech, and low-latency applications that demand high availability and carrier density
  • Compliance-forward industries (healthcare, payment processing) needing certified infrastructure

name: IAD-04 Data Center
description:
Located in Ashburn, Virginia, Aligned Data Centers’ IAD-04 facility is positioned in the heart of the Northern Virginia data center corridor, the world’s largest and most interconnected cloud and colocation market.

Strategically situated for low-latency access to major cloud hubs and key East Coast enterprise markets, the site benefits from robust regional fiber infrastructure and proximity to Washington D.C.

Designed for scalable hyperscale and enterprise deployments, IAD-04 combines sustainable engineering with adaptive infrastructure for flexible capacity expansion.

The facility resides in a low-seismic-risk zone and leverages diverse utility feeds for exceptional reliability and redundancy.

Purpose-built for cloud, AI/ML, and high-density workloads, Aligned’s IAD-04 positions itself as a leading-edge data center for hyperscale, enterprise, and cloud deployments.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Power Capacity: Up to 120 MW of critical load capacity designed to support high-density compute environments.
  • Cooling Efficiency: Incorporates Aligned’s patented Delta³™ cooling technology for adaptive cooling, enabling high-density and liquid-ready deployments with industry-leading PUE.
  • Redundancy: Engineered with 2N and N+1 configurations across power and cooling for continuous uptime and operational resilience.
  • Sustainability: Access to renewable and sustainable power options within Dominion Energy’s green energy programs; infrastructure optimized for energy efficiency and reduced carbon footprint.
  • Scalability: Modular and rapidly deployable architecture allows customers to scale seamlessly from kilowatts to megawatts.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Multi-layered physical and electronic security includes biometric authentication, CCTV surveillance, mantrap entry, and 24×7 on-site security personnel.
  • Comprehensive fire detection and suppression systems with VESDA early smoke detection and multi-zone fire containment.
  • Compliant with key industry standards such as ISO 27001, ISO 9001, ISO 50001, SOC 1 Type II, SOC 2 Type II, and PCI DSS.
  • The facility upholds 100% uptime SLAs, ensuring mission-critical workloads remain secure and continuously available.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral data center offering access to regional, national, and international network providers.
  • Direct connectivity to Ashburn’s major internet exchanges and cloud on-ramps for AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure, and Oracle Cloud.
  • Supports dark fiber, lit services, and cross-connects, enabling low-latency, diverse routing options and network redundancy.
  • Proximity to major peering hubs and subsea cable access points, ensuring optimized performance for global traffic exchange.

💼 Who It Serves

  • Hyperscale cloud and AI/ML platforms requiring sustainable, scalable infrastructure in the core U.S. interconnection hub.
  • Enterprises and SaaS providers seeking secure, compliant environments for hybrid and multi-cloud strategies.
  • Telecommunications carriers and service providers expanding low-latency network presence.
  • Media, gaming, and content delivery platforms with bandwidth-intensive workloads.
  • Government, financial, and healthcare organizations prioritizing uptime, compliance, and proximity to the D.C. metro.

name: Denver- Parker Data Center
description:
The Flexential Parker Data Center, located at the corner of Chambers Road and Compark Blvd. in Parker, Colorado, is Flexential’s newest purpose-built facility in the Denver metro area. Positioned just one mile from the Flexential Englewood data center, Parker expands regional coverage with 22.5 MW of critical load capacity across 249,000 square feet, powered by Core Electric Cooperative.

Strategically situated in the technology-driven Denver market, Parker provides enterprises with high-density colocation, cloud, and interconnection services. This facility strengthens application response times for distributed users in the Rocky Mountain region while offering scalability for organizations in industries such as finance, logistics, healthcare IT, and mobility.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • 22.5 MW of critical power capacity, with 4.5 MW per data hall
  • Distributed redundant (4:3) UPS topology, with 5-minute battery reserve
  • 55 MW of standby power generation
  • Cooling capacity: 25 MW, N+2 redundancy
  • Liquid-cooling ready, designed for high-density environments from day one
  • 249,000 sq. ft. footprint on 17 acres
  • 100% SLA on power, network, and bandwidth
  • Dedicated customer amenities: meeting rooms, office lease space, and business continuity suites

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 5-zone physical security access model
  • 24/7 onsite security and technical staff
  • Dual-factor authentication with biometric scanners
  • CCTV surveillance, monitored intercom systems, and cage-level cameras
  • PCI DSS compliant site
  • Annual independent audits: SOC 1 Type II, SOC 2 Type II, SOC 3, NIST 800-53, ITAR: SSAE 18
  • HITRUST and PCI certification services available

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral environment with access to a rich ecosystem of carriers and cloud providers
  • Connections to strategic carrier hotels and 100 Gbps network backbone
  • One unified fabric for on-demand connectivity provisioning
  • IP transit service with Tier I carrier blend and basic DDoS scrubbing
  • Point-to-point and mesh services to connect Flexential data centers nationwide

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises in finance, healthcare, mobility, logistics, and retail requiring high-density infrastructure
  • Businesses adopting hybrid IT strategies with colocation, private, public, and compliant cloud solutions
  • Organizations with strict compliance requirements (PCI DSS, HITRUST, NIST)
  • Customers needing disaster recovery, business continuity, and professional IT services such as migration and risk management
  • Companies prioritizing secure, scalable, and liquid-cooling-ready infrastructure in the Denver technology market

name: CO1 Aurora Data Center
description:
The CO1 Aurora Data Center, located at 3431 N. Windsor Drive, Aurora, Colorado, is a purpose-built colocation and network facility operated by 365 Data Centers. Situated about 15 miles east of downtown Denver and approximately 20 minutes from Denver International Airport, the site serves as a strategic business continuity, disaster recovery, and edge hub for the Mountain West region. 

Its proximity to Denver’s metro fiber infrastructure, combined with carrier-rich connectivity, positions CO1 Aurora as a low-latency, resilient option for enterprises, cloud providers, and network operators seeking a strong presence in Colorado’s data center ecosystem.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Facility size: 150,000 sq ft gross data center footprint 
  • Raised-floor / colocation space: ~ 45,000 sq ft of raised floor dedicated for colocation use 
  • Power capacity: 8 MW allocated to the data center
  • Redundant UPS: 2N UPS configuration with 16 total units (redundant) 
  • Generator redundancy: N+1 generator system with 34,000-gallon fuel tank
  • Cooling: 1,000-ton cooling plant with N+1 redundant cooling systems, supporting overhead and underfloor airflow 
  • Fire protection: Dry-pipe pre-action sprinklers in raised areas and critical space; wet systems in administrative zones 
  • Raised floor height: 24 inches (with subfloor cabling and fire / flood sensors) 
  • Loading dock: weather-protected dock with lift gate suited for full-size trailers 
  • Hot-aisle / cold-aisle layout with cold-aisle containment capabilities

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Multi-layer security with 24/7/365 infrastructure and perimeter monitoring 
  • Access controls: layered physical access security systems, controlled entry 
  • Surveillance: Over 90 internal and external cameras with CCTV recording and ~90-day retention 
  • On-site presence: 24/7 customer support, staff, and Remote Hands service by trained technicians 
  • Certifications: The CO1 Aurora listing includes HIPAA, PCI DSS, SOC 2 Type II, SSAE 18 as reported in third-party directories. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier ecosystem: On-site connectivity to major network operators including AT&T, Cogent, Comcast, Lumen (CenturyLink & Level 3 & TimeWarner), Verizon (XO), Zayo 
  • Network reach: Facility supports over 275 carrier network PoPs (points of presence)
  • Fiber diversity: Multiple fiber entry points and path redundancy to support resilient connectivity
  • Interconnect / peering: Supports cross-connects, interconnection services, and integration into 365’s broader network of data centers & cloud on-ramps 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises and cloud providers that require a geographically strategic colocation presence in Colorado / Mountain West
  • Organizations needing a resilient disaster recovery / business continuity site near Denver
  • Telecom, ISP, and network operators looking for carrier-rich interconnection in the region
  • Workloads sensitive to latency (financial services, real-time applications, content delivery)
  • Companies with compliance or regulated workloads that require certified infrastructure options

name: T-Mobile East Wenatchee
description:
The T-Mobile East Wenatchee data center (also referred to as Polaris, located on Sabey Data Centers’ Intergate.Columbia campus in East Wenatchee, Washington) is a key facility leased by T-Mobile. It is part of a larger multi-tenant campus operated by Sabey, situated at 4405 Grant Rd, East Wenatchee, WA 98802. 

Strategically, the facility benefits from the Pacific Northwest’s abundant, low-cost, renewable hydroelectric power; favorable climate; seismic stability; and proximity to major West Coast metropolitan and cloud hub markets, which helps ensure low latency to Seattle and other tech centers. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Capacity & Scalability: Sabey’s East Wenatchee campus (Intergate.Columbia) is expanding. As of 2025, ~5.5 MW of Tier III-standard critical power will be available in the East Wenatchee facility starting November 2025, with more MW planned for late 2026 and early 2028. 
  • Power Costs & Source: Power is supplied by Douglas County Public Utility District via sustainable hydroelectric sources, offering some of the lowest electrical rates in the U.S. (~ US$0.05/kWh). 
  • Energy Efficiency: The campus has an average PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) of ~1.2. 
  • Mechanical / Backup Infrastructure:
  • Generators capable of 2.75 MW with 72-hour run time at peak load. 
  • Redundant electrical systems with multiple carriers and fiber entry points
  • Redundant HVAC / cooling systems designed for immediate failover.
  • Modular Design & Space Options: Offers colocation, powered shell (“powered shell” meaning the shell is delivered with power, water, fiber, etc.), and build-to-suit options. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical Security: Perimeter security including fencing, setbacks and berms. Access controls with multi-tier measures. 
  • On‐Site Staff / Operations: On-site technicians and a “world-class operations team” present to ensure reliability, maintenance, critical infrastructure monitoring, and to provide remote-hands support. 
  • Resilience / Redundancy: Redundant mechanical, electrical, HVAC systems to support uninterrupted operation. Backup power generators with fuel supplies on-site and through emergency fuel contracts.
  • Compliance / Standards: The facility is referred to as Tier III-standard critical power capacity in recent expansion announcements. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-Neutral Campus: The campus supports multiple telecommunications carriers; customers have choices for connectivity. 
  • Major Carriers Supported: Includes AT&T, Spectrum/Charter, Verizon, Ziply Fiber, Zayo, NoaNet, Douglas County PUD, among others. 
  • Fiber Entry & Interconnectivity: Redundant fiber paths, cloud on-ramps, and interconnection services as part of Sabey’s offering.

💼 Who It Serves

  • Telecommunications providers — e.g. T-Mobile itself, benefiting from the facility for network, switching, and data handling infrastructure.
  • Enterprises needing low cost of ownership and high power reliability — especially those sensitive to power costs, latency, sustainability (e.g. tech companies, cloud providers) in the Pacific Northwest region.
  • Organizations needing scalable, efficient colocation or build-to-suit solutions — companies requiring redundancy, modular design, and flexible deployment.
  • Sustainability-focused customers — interested in renewable power, low PUE, environmentally favorable operations.
full_address: 4405 Grant Rd, East Wenatchee, WA 98802, EE. UU.

name: SDC Seattle Data Center
description:
SDC Seattle, operated by Sabey Data Centers, is a flagship data center campus located in Tukwila, WA, just minutes south of downtown Seattle and approximately 3 miles from Sea-Tac International Airport.  The facility leverages 100% carbon-free hydropower supplied by Seattle City Light and “free cooling” for much of the year, delivering a sustainable, low total cost of ownership solution. With over 1,200,000 square feet of mission-critical space, it supports colocation, powered shell, and build-to-suit configurations. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Total campus power capacity: 54 MW of critical power delivered to the campus. 
  • New Tier III-standard critical power: 6 MW coming online December 1, 2025. 
  • Average annualized Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of approximately 1.4-1.6 (or roughly ~1.5) depending on build-out. 
  • Floor-to-ceiling height: up to 22 ft ceiling height; data halls typically offer ~14 ft drop ceiling with ~8 ft plenum above. 
  • Cooling systems: utilizes free cooling most of the year (leveraging ambient Pacific Northwest climate), hot-aisle/cold-aisle containment, evaporative and economizer cooling; liquid cooling-ready capacity planned. 
  • Redundant mechanical and electrical systems: backup generators with 72-hour run time at peak load; on-site fuel supply plus emergency fuel contracts. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical security features include perimeter setbacks, berms, fencing; secure access checkpoints; man traps at building entrances; biometric locks; ballistic-rated access points.
  • 24/7 security personnel; CCTV (closed circuit cameras) across campus. 
  • Fire detection systems above and below racks. 
  • Critical environment monitoring and control systems; online customer portal for real-time reporting. 
  • Compliance / certifications: HIPAA, HITECH, LEED, PCI DSS, SOC 1 Type 2, SOC 2 Type 2, SSAE-18. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with more than 20 major carriers for networking redundancy and cloud-on-ramps. 
  • Dual campus fiber entries and redundant fiber pathways. 
  • On-site, carrier-class Meet-Me-Rooms.
  • Low-latency access to regional hubs; direct link to Seattle’s Westin Building. 

💼 Who It Serves

name: 165 Halsey Street Data Center
description:
165 Halsey Street has a notable history as one of the premier telecommunications locations in the US. Like any successful entity in the telecom industry, the building has more than well adapted to the needs of its customers and to the telecom landscape throughout its existence.

Perfectly Designed and Adaptable for a Premium Data Center

In 1996, 165 Halsey Street was a 1.2M sq ft completely vacant department store (formerly Bamberger’s) when it was purchased by a well-capitalized and successful investment group.

Simultaneously, Tishman Real Estate Services had been engaged by MFS Intelenet, one of the original Competitive Local Exchange Carriers (CLECs) which grew out of the Telecommunications Act of 1996, deregulating the industry. 165 Halsey Street proximity to an abundance of fiber providers, access to ample power and building intrinsics of large floor plates, heavy floor loads, good column spacing, tremendous riser capabilities, large open roof space for generators and cooling equipment and large basement areas for fuel tanks plus an accommodating ownership provided the perfect opportunity for MFS to locate a major switch facility onto an entire floor of over 87,500 sf.

Recognizing the opportunity that there would be more demand for a well designed and organized dedicated telecom/data center building, the ownership assembled a team consisting of Tishman Real Estate Services as a development advisor and leasing and licensing agent. Jaros Baum and Bolles as MEP consulting engineers, Tishman Construction as construction manager, Aschettino as the structural engineer and Rosenbaum Design Group as the architect. This same team has been working continuously on the building for over 18 years and has overseen the design, construction, leasing and licensing of the entire building.

In 2000, the building’s first Meet me Room was created to accommodate the interconnection needs of the Telecom tenancies. This has since morphed into a first-class, full-service colocation business called 165 Halsey Colocation of over 180,000 sq ft.

Seattle City Light's utility rates are some of the most attractive in the country with average annual costs per kWh of less than $0.07. All systems are meticulously monitored and maintained by our engineering team.

H5 Data Centers innovative electrical and mechanical architecture achieve up to 40% better energy efficiency than other data centers. The Seattle data center offers a leading Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of ~1.35, ensuring optimal energy utilization and reduced waste.

Electrical and mechanical highlights include:

  • N+1 UPS distribution
  • N+1 mechanical systems that incorporate outside air "free cooling"
  • Hot and cold row data center design
  • Industry-leading Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of ~1.35

The Seattle data center is a highly secure data center to meet the stringent regulatory requirements of our customer base. The Seattle data center is equipped with on-site security staffing, mantraps, biometric and keycard access systems, and digital security cameras.

name: Houston One Data Center
description:
Houston One Data Center, operated by Element Critical (formerly Skybox Houston One), is a purpose-built, high-density colocation and interconnection facility located at 22000 Franz Road, Katy, Texas, in the Houston Energy Corridor.

Strategically positioned along the I-10 fiber-optic corridor, it offers ultra-low latency connectivity to central Houston business hubs.The facility is built to LEED Gold standards, with a reinforced FM I-150 roof and structural design able to resist hurricane-grade winds of 190 mph, ensuring resiliency in a region prone to severe weather. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Total building size: 98,200 sq ft 
  • Colocation (white space) area: 48,500 sq ft 
  • Power capacity: 26 MW utility feed
  • Power redundancy: N+2 on critical loads, dual converter design, and premium rollover switching between substation transformers
  • Backup power: 2N diesel generator configuration, redundant fuel pumps, on-site fuel storage (e.g. DH4 hall: 30,000 gallons) 
  • Cooling: CRAH/CRAC systems, with direct water-cooled chillers in N+1 design, with dedicated CRAH galleries (outside data hall) 
  • High-density support: up to 50 kW per rack and liquid- or immersion-cooling ready 
  • Structural resilience: shell constructed with tilt-up, 6″ concrete roof deck, FM I-150 rating, exterior built to sustain 190 mph wind loads 
  • Campus & site: built over ~10 acres, loop-fed connection to a 400 MVA substation, dual underground utility conduits to each hall 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Tier III concurrently maintainable design certification 
  • Exterior & perimeter: gated Ameristar crash-rated fencing, ballistic-rated exterior glass, 7 layers of security 
  • Access control: 2FA biometric authentication, mantraps, motion detectors, card access systems 
  • Surveillance: interior and exterior CCTV with 90-day video retention 
  • Certifications & audits: ISAE 3402, SOC 1 Type II, SOC 2 Type II, NERC CIP compliance 
  • Fire & life safety: pre-action dual interlock dry pipe sprinkler systems, nitrogen generation for zones, VESDA smoke detection, wet sprinklers for non-critical spaces 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with a robust meet-me room and redundant internal network fabrics 
  • Over 17 on-net carriers including AT&T, Comcast, Crown Castle, Lumen, Verizon, Zayo, FiberLight, and others 
  • Dual diverse fiber entry paths in concrete-encased duct banks for resilience 
  • Ultra-low latency to Houston’s I-10 fiber backbone and to 1301 Fannin carrier hotel 
  • Cloud On-Ramp and hybrid cloud connectivity: bridging customers’ private and public clouds seamlessly 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises in energy, oil & gas, finance, and industrial sectors in the Greater Houston region
  • Cloud providers, hyperscalers, and service providers needing high-density, low-latency infrastructure
  • Businesses requiring build-to-suit data halls, suites, or mission-critical colocation
  • Organizations needing hybrid IT and seamless connectivity to public clouds
  • Customers needing resilient infrastructure in a hurricane-prone region — with high structural and operational safeguards
full_address: 22000 Franz Road, Katy, TX, USA

name: Chicago Data Center
description:
Welcome to Chicago, IL - the hub of global trade and connectivity. Our facility boasts 100 MW of utility power, delivered through 12.47 kV circuits from 3 underground substations. In addition, our 150 MW generator power capacity and low cost of electricity guarantee reliable and affordable service.

But it's not just about power. We offer dedicated access to an extensive ecosystem of carriers, mobile operators, cloud and content providers, both within our building and throughout the metro area, ensuring the highest performance for your mission-critical applications. Our IPv6- and MPLS-enabled 10-GbE private global IP network, along with three diverse fiber paths to our facility in Elk Grove Village, IL, further enhance connectivity options.

We take security seriously, with 24/7 on-site staffing, 2-factor authentication, and biometric access control. Our CCTV monitoring ensures constant surveillance for added peace of mind.

Choose us for unrivaled connectivity, power, and security.

name: T-Mobile 401 N Broad St
description:
401 North Broad Street (“401 N Broad”) is a major carrier-neutral interconnection and colocation data center facility located in Center City Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It occupies the historic Terminal Commerce Building, a large Art Deco historic complex with over 1.3 million gross square feet.Owned and operated by Netrality Properties, 401 N Broad is one of the most fiber-dense and network-rich facilities on the U.S. Northeast corridor between New York and Virginia. Its positioning in Center City provides strategic advantages: dense metro and long-haul fiber routes, proximity to major business, financial, healthcare, and academic institutions, and easy access for on-site staffing and cross-connects.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Total Building Size: ~ 1,300,000 sq ft in the Terminal Commerce structure. 
  • Power Infrastructure: Utility feed capacity about 40,000 kW, backup generator power ~ 4,000 kW N+1, UPS system rated at ~ 1,200 kVA with 2N redundancy. 
  • Cooling Systems: Condenser water systems (~3,600 tons capacity) in N+1 redundancy, HVAC/data room CRAC units, cold-aisle containment.
  • Structural / Physical Features: Floor loading capacity ~ 200 lb/sq ft (center to center), ceiling heights slab-to-slab ~ 12 ft in many areas, overhead cable ladder racks, secure loading docks, freight elevators (capacity ~ 10,000 lbs). Building is outside the 100-year floodplain. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical Security Measures: On-site 24/7 network operations center (NOC), keycard access, biometric access, mantraps, secure multi-bay loading dock, CCTV surveillance systems.
  • Monitoring & Staffing: 24/7 staffed operations; critical facility monitoring system. 
  • Compliance Certifications: The facility is compliant with HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC Audit standards (e.g. SSAE-16 / SOC 1, SOC 2) according to multiple listings. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-Neutral Status: Yes. Netrality’s 401 N Broad is a major carrier hotel / interconnection hub. 
  • On-Site Carriers / Providers: Over 70 unique network providers are on-net. Tenants include Zayo, Lumen, Crown Castle, XO (among others). 
  • Fiber Entry / Diversity: Six diverse Points of Entry (POEs) for fiber into the building; pre-built innerducts from POEs to the Meet-Me Room; multiple risers. 
  • Meet-Me Room (MMR): Yes, the facility includes a Meet-Me Room for tenant/provider interconnection. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises in finance, healthcare, education, and life sciences seeking highly interconnected, secure, and regulated data infrastructure in the mid-Atlantic / Northeast corridor. 
  • Network operators, cloud providers, content delivery networks (CDNs), managed service providers (MSPs), and telecom carriers who need rich fiber density, carrier neutrality, and numerous interconnection options. 
  • Organizations requiring compliance certifications (e.g. HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC) and high availability infrastructure for critical workloads.
full_address: 401 North Broad Street, Philadelphia, PA 19108, USA

name: Orlando Location

  • Total Space: Over 150,000 sq ft of modular, scalable white space

Cooling & Sustainability:

Security & Compliance:

  • Fully compliant with SOC 2 Type II, PCI DSS, HIPAA, and ISO/IEC 27001

Certifications:

  • SOC 1 Type II, SOC 2 Type II
  • PCI DSS
  • Uptime Institute Tier III Design & Constructed Facility Certified

Strategic Role:
