name: EDGED DES MOINES DATA CENTER
description:
Edged Des Moines is a state-of-the-art data center facility being developed by Edged (a subsidiary of Endeavour) in Ankeny, Iowa, just north of Des Moines. Designed for high-density AI training and inference workloads, this facility leverages advanced waterless cooling and ultra-efficient energy systems. Its strategic location offers central U.S. positioning, reliable and affordable power, and strong fiber connectivity, making it a key infrastructure asset in the growing Midwest tech hub. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Critical Capacity: 13.2 MW 
  • Size: approx 105,000 square feet (≈ 9,755 m²) facility on a 17.5-acre site 
  • Cooling: ThermalWorks closed-loop waterless cooling system; supports up to 70 kW/rack with air cooling, 200 kW/rack with plug-and-play liquid cooling integration. 
  • Water conservation: expected to save over 52 million gallons of water per year compared to a similar conventional data center. 
  • Energy efficiency: Target PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) of ~1.15 portfolio-wide for Edged. 
  • Build: single-story building; includes two loading dock bays; two meet-me rooms; full site utility infrastructure. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Tier III design allowing for concurrent maintainability ensuring high uptime and resilience. 
  • Physical access 24×7×365. 
  • Dual interlock pre-action sprinkler system; addressable smoke detectors with hot aisle early detection. 
  • Standby generators using Tier 4-Final engines, which reduce NOx and particulate emissions up to ~99%. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Multiple fiber providers with four diverse points of entry. 
  • Two meet-me rooms on-site, enabling interconnection options.
  • Location benefits: proximity to Des Moines, which has strong fiber and backbone infrastructure connecting to both coasts and other U.S. metro regions. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Hyperscale cloud providers needing large compute capacity, especially for AI training/inference workloads. 
  • Large enterprises requiring high-density compute and sustainable, energy-efficient infrastructure. 
  • Technology companies seeking capacity in the Midwest with low latency to both coasts and central U.S. markets.
full_address: 9100 Southeast Fourmile Drive, Ankeny, Iowa, EE. UU.

name: ATL01-02 Data Center
description:
Edged ATL01-02 (also known as Atlanta01-02) is a state-of-the-art data center facility operated by Edged Energy, located at 1760 Thomas Street NW, Atlanta, Georgia. 

It sits on Edged’s Atlanta campus which spans over 80 acres near Downtown Atlanta — a region well positioned as a technology and interconnection hub in the Southeast U.S.

Strategically, ATL01-02 offers substantial critical IT power capacity and modern infrastructure, making it appealing for high-density workloads, cloud, AI, and hybrid deployments. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Part of a multi-building campus with a total capacity of ~169 MW for critical load across the Atlanta site. 
  • The ATL01-02 building delivers 100 MW of power capacity.
  • White space / data-hall area: ~181,744 sq ft with 17 data halls in a single-story facility. 
  • Highly modular designs: supports air cooling up to ~70 kW per rack, and plug-and-play liquid cooling allowing densities up to 200 kW per rack. 
  • Cooling: uses ThermalWorks closed-loop systems, rooftop air-cooled chillers, and non-toxic, low-global-warming potential refrigerants (like R1234ze or R515-B). 
  • Designed for leading energy efficiency: portfolio-wide design PUE ~ 1.15, and zero water consumption for cooling. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7/365 secure access to facility.
  • Onsite continuous monitoring of power, mechanical, and electrical systems via a secure DCIM (Data Center Infrastructure Management) platform. 
  • Advanced fire detection & suppression: data halls have double-interlock pre-action mist systems; addressable smoke detectors; hot-aisle early smoke detection. 
  • Designed under Tier III standards (“concurrently maintainable”) with N+1 redundancy in power, UPS, cooling systems.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Multiple fiber providers available onsite, with four diverse physical points of fiber entry into the facility. 
  • Carrier-neutral facility offering interconnection services; suitable for colocation, cloud on-ramps, multi-hybrid cloud deployments. 
  • Meet-me rooms present; leading Network Service Providers (NSPs) and multiple cloud providers are accessible. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises requiring high-performance compute, AI/ML workloads, or high-density infrastructure demands. 
  • Cloud service providers and hybrid cloud operators seeking low latency, high reliability in the Southeast U.S. 
  • Companies with stringent compliance, uptime, and energy efficiency mandates (e.g. financial services, healthcare, technology firms) that benefit from the facility’s Tier III design, redundancy, and efficiency.
full_address: 1760 Thomas Street Northwest, Atlanta, GA 30318, EE. UU.

name: TX11 - San Antonio Data Center
description:
The Vantage Data Centers – TX11 facility is a large-scale data center development located at 14720 Omicron Drive, San Antonio, Texas, part of the Far West-Side (Texas Research Park) region of Bexar County. Positioned in a strategic data-centre corridor near major fiber and power infrastructure, this campus is designed to support growing demand for hyperscale, colocation and cloud infrastructure in the U.S. South-Central market.

As part of Vantage’s strategic expansion into Texas, the TX11 site underscores the company’s commitment to scale, connectivity, and future-proof infrastructure in a region that is rapidly emerging as a major digital-infrastructure hub.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • The building is planned as a two-story facility spanning approximately 242,667 sq ft (≈ 22,545 m²).
  • The project cost is estimated at approximately US$96 million.
  • The permitted IT load is approximately 32 MW, configured as four data halls each rated ~8 MW.
  • Construction began in December 2023 with an estimated completion date around January 2026.
  • Modular and scalable design supports future expansion or densification as demand evolves.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • The site will include Vantage’s standard physical security infrastructure: 24×7 on-site security, biometric access, card-based authentication, man-trap entries, perimeter fencing, vehicle barriers and CCTV coverage.
  • Utility feeds and mechanical systems are designed for redundancy and high availability, consistent with enterprise-grade operations.
  • Operational protocols align with global standards (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, PCI DSS) and continuous infrastructure monitoring via BMS/DCIM.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Located in a carrier-neutral environment in San Antonio’s Far West Side, the site provides multiple fiber entry points and diverse network routes to support low latency and redundancy.
  • Designed to host wholesale, colocation and hybrid-cloud workloads, enabling access to cloud on-ramps, dark fiber and cross-connects.
  • San Antonio’s infrastructure advantages (abundant land, power and fiber availability) make the TX11 facility a strategic option for regional expansion beyond congested coastal markets.

💼 Who It Serves

The TX11 facility is a fit for a broad spectrum of customers:

  • Hyperscale cloud and AI / ML providers seeking ~30 MW-level capacity with scalable infrastructure.
  • Enterprises and SaaS firms needing secure colocation, hybrid-cloud or private-cloud platforms in a growth region.
  • Telecom, content delivery or edge-infrastructure providers expanding in Texas and the broader South-Central U.S.
  • AI, HPC and analytics clients requiring efficient, scalable compute infrastructure with solid connectivity.
  • Regulated industries (financial services, government, healthcare) that require U.S.-based, compliant, resilient hosting environments.
full_address: 14720 Omicron Dr, San Antonio, Texas 78245, USA

name: Westlake Data Center
description:
The Vantage Data Centers – Westlake campus is a next-generation hyperscale data center development located in South Fulton, Georgia, part of the greater Atlanta metropolitan region. Strategically positioned west of Plummer Road and north of Riverside Drive, the Westlake campus leverages proximity to Atlanta’s major fiber corridors, robust utility infrastructure, and transportation networks, ensuring low-latency connectivity, reliable power, and scalability for large enterprises and cloud providers.

As part of Vantage’s continued investment in the southeastern U.S., the Westlake project reinforces the company’s position as a leader in sustainable, high-capacity data infrastructure. The facility is purpose-built to support hyperscale cloud, AI, and enterprise workloads, meeting the rapidly increasing digital demands across the Atlanta market.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

The Westlake Data Center is being designed as a large-scale, future-ready campus, offering flexibility and operational excellence:

  • Planned for up to 96 MW of critical IT load upon full campus build-out, distributed across multiple data center facilities.
  • Occupies approximately 754,220 square feet of total building area, with phased development to support future expansion.
  • Built to Tier III+ design standards, featuring N+1 redundancy across all critical power and cooling systems.
  • Incorporates closed-loop chilled water systems and airside economization for efficient thermal management and reduced PUE.
  • Designed for sustainability and environmental efficiency, including low-water usage, renewable energy options, and a target PUE of <1.3.
  • Modular and scalable construction methodology enables rapid deployment for hyperscale and enterprise tenants.

🔐 Security & Compliance

Vantage Data Centers upholds a comprehensive, multi-layered security framework at the Westlake campus to protect customer infrastructure and data integrity:

  • 24×7×365 on-site security personnel, reinforced with biometric access control, proximity card systems, and man-trap entry points.
  • Full perimeter fencing, vehicle crash barriers, intrusion detection, and CCTV video surveillance across all facility zones.
  • Compliance with ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, and PCI DSS standards for operational and data security assurance.
  • Continuous monitoring through Building Management Systems (BMS) and Data Center Infrastructure Management (DCIM) for proactive performance oversight.
  • Sustainability and governance measures aligned with Vantage’s corporate commitment to carbon-neutral operations.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

The Westlake Data Center is built for high network diversity and redundancy, making it a prime interconnection hub within the Southeast region:

  • Carrier-neutral design offering diverse fiber routes and multiple points of entry for enhanced connectivity and fault tolerance.
  • Direct access to Tier-1 carriers, cloud on-ramps, and regional Internet exchanges, enabling high-performance hybrid and multi-cloud deployments.
  • Supports dark fiber, cross-connects, and private interconnects for customers requiring customized connectivity solutions.
  • Proximity to Atlanta’s major peering hubs ensures low-latency connectivity throughout the southeastern United States and beyond.

💼 Who It Serves

⚙️ Facility Highlights —

  • 5 MW+ of critical IT load capacity currently available with planned expansion to support much higher power demands.

💼 Who It Serves —

name: Phoenix Data Center
description:
H5 Data Centers' Phoenix Data Center is Ideal for Companies Seeking Private, Secure Data Center Suites

H5 Data Centers' Phoenix data center is a purpose-built, state-of-the-art Tier III facility designed to provide industry-leading energy efficiency and privacy. The data center is located in the Arizona Technology Corridor and can total up to 180,000 square feet and 20 MWs. Now with nearly 40 customers, the Phoenix data center can accommodate the mission critical needs of leading technology and Fortune 500 companies.

The Phoenix data center is designed to enable scalable and modular growth of IT requirements. Easily deploy cabinets and IT architecture in a consistent, repeatable fashion at H5 Data Centers' Phoenix data center. Since being built from the ground-up, the Phoenix data center boasts a 100% uptime track record.

Conveniently located in Chandler's high-tech corridor, H5 Data Centers' Phoenix data center is in close proximity to the Phoenix International Airport and interstate highways.

Unique aspects of the Phoenix data center include:

  • 180,000 SF data center campus
  • Private data center suites with dedicated mechanical fire suppression systems
  • 24x7 on-site engineering and security team
  • Available office, staging, storage and conference room space
  • Ample, on-site parking
  • Arizona Technology Corridor location
Power

SRP provides the Phoenix data center with up to 30 MWs of reliable and affordable power (average annual costs per kWh of less than $0.07) from diverse substations. All systems are meticulously maintained by our 24x7 on-site Phoenix engineering team.

H5 Data Centers' innovative electrical and mechanical architecture achieve up to 40% better energy efficiency than other data centers. The Phoenix data center offers a leading Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) of less than 1.35 and doesn't use water, ensuring optimal energy utilization and reduced waste.

Electrical and mechanical highlights include:

  • Diverse utility feeds from SRPs high capacity, modern power grid
  • Concurrently maintainable, Tier III data center design
  • N+1 UPS distribution
  • N+1 emergency generator farm
  • Dedicated N+1 mechanical systems
  • Isolated hot and cold row data center design
  • Waterless mechanical system design
  • Ability for customer customization of temperature and humidity controls, including 100% outside air capability
  • Cabinet densities of more than 20 KWs per rack
  • Customer monitoring of power usage
  • Industry-leading PUE
  • Less than $0.07/kwh avg. annual power pricing from SRP
Security

The Phoenix data center is highly secure, featuring a 22-foot wall surrounding the facility to ensure maximum security. To compliment the perimeter wall, the Phoenix data center also includes 24x7 security staffing, mantraps with anti-tailgating technology, biometric and keycard access systems, and digital security cameras. Each data center suite is constructed of steel and concrete and features a digital camera at each opening.

The data center offers the following security benefits:

  • Mantraps
  • Biometric and key card access controls
  • 22-foot wall surrounding the data center
  • 20+ on-site cameras monitored 24x7
  • Dedicated fire suppression system
  • Early smoke detection system
  • Annual Type 2 SOC 1 audits
  • Annual ISO 27001 certification
  • Easy PCI compliance
Network

H5 Data Centers' Phoenix data center offers carrier-neutral data center services, providing customers with access to a variety of different regional, national and international telecommunications carriers and fiber providers. With its carrier partners, H5 serves a growing community of enterprises, content delivery networks (CDNs) and cloud service providers seeking to improve their mission critical operations.

Highlights of H5 Data Centers' Phoenix network-neutral data center include:

  • Access to the regions top metro fiber and communications providers
  • Diverse fiber points of entry
  • Cross connections available within 2 business days of order
Features

Phoenix is one of the top disaster recovery locations in the United States, as it is very unlikely to be affected by tornadoes, earthquakes, hurricanes, or flooding. For decades, Phoenix has been a destination for national enterprises looking for a safe and secure site to locate a data center.

H5 Data Centers conducts annual Type 2 SOC 1 audits and ISO 27001 certification.

For larger customers, H5 Data Centers offers the ability to qualify for Arizonas progressive sales & use tax abatement program. If a customer has a requirement that ultimately is equal or greater to 500 KWs, then it may be eligible to receive 100% Arizona-state sales & use taxes. This program is indicative of Arizona and Chandlers attractive business-friendly attitude.

H5 Data Centers' Phoenix data center product offering includes:

  • Turn-key wholesale data center space
  • Private data center suites
  • Private cages and cabinets
  • Powered shell build-outs
  • Office and storage space
  • 24x7 remote hands services
  • 100% uptime guarantee

name: TX21 - San Antonio Data Center
description:
The Vantage Data Centers – TX21 campus is a large-scale hyperscale data center located in San Antonio, Texas, within Bexar County. Situated at 5207 Rogers Road, the TX21 facility is positioned in one of the fastest-growing data center markets in the southern United States, supported by strong fiber infrastructure, stable power delivery, and a business-friendly environment.

As part of Vantage’s continued expansion across high-demand U.S. regions, the TX21 site demonstrates the company’s commitment to scalable, sustainable, and future-ready infrastructure. Designed for hyperscale, enterprise, and AI workloads, this campus adds significant capacity to the South-Central U.S. technology corridor, complementing Vantage’s growing national footprint.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

The TX21 San Antonio Data Center is a multi-phase development built to deliver high capacity, redundancy, and operational resilience:

  • The initial phase encompasses approximately 360,000 square feet of purpose-built data center space.
  • The facility is designed as a three-story structure, with two levels of shell space reserved for future data halls and one level active for initial operations.
  • Construction for the first phase began in late 2023, with completion targeted for mid-2025.
  • The estimated project investment for this phase is approximately $157 million.
  • The campus is carrier-neutral, offering multiple fiber entry points and diverse network routes to ensure high availability and redundancy.
  • TX21 is engineered for Tier III+ reliability, featuring N+1 redundancy across power and cooling systems.
  • Sustainability is a core design element, including energy-efficient mechanical systems and low-water-use cooling technologies consistent with Vantage’s global sustainability goals.

🔐 Security & Compliance

Vantage Data Centers maintains global best practices for physical and digital security across all campuses, including TX21:

  • 24×7×365 on-site security personnel, with biometric access controls, keycard authentication, and man-trap entry points.
  • Perimeter fencing, vehicle barriers, and CCTV monitoring provide complete coverage of all access zones.
  • Built to comply with ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, and PCI DSS operational standards.
  • Continuous infrastructure oversight through Building Management Systems (BMS) and Data Center Infrastructure Management (DCIM) platforms.
  • Fire suppression, redundant electrical systems, and fault-tolerant design ensure maximum uptime and business continuity.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

TX21 is strategically located within San Antonio’s expanding data center corridor, providing excellent connectivity and accessibility:

  • The site supports multiple carriers and fiber providers, ensuring path diversity and low-latency connectivity.
  • Carrier-neutral interconnection options support hybrid and multi-cloud environments for enterprises and hyperscale tenants.
  • Dark fiber, cross-connects, and private interconnects are available for direct cloud and data exchange connections.
  • San Antonio’s strong power infrastructure, abundant land availability, and geographic proximity to major U.S. network routes make it an ideal location for large-scale data center operations.

💼 Who It Serves

The Vantage TX21 San Antonio campus is designed to serve a wide range of customers and use cases:

  • Hyperscale cloud and AI providers requiring high-density, scalable infrastructure in the South-Central U.S.
  • Enterprises and SaaS platforms seeking secure colocation and hybrid-cloud solutions with regional redundancy.
  • Telecommunications and content delivery providers expanding their network presence in Texas and surrounding markets.
  • AI, HPC, and analytics organizations demanding sustainable, high-performance infrastructure for compute-intensive workloads.
  • Government, healthcare, and financial institutions requiring secure, compliant, and U.S.-based hosting environments.

name: SDC Columbia Data Center
description:
The SDC Columbia campus, operated by Sabey Data Centers, is located in East Wenatchee, Washington. Strategically positioned in Central Washington, this facility benefits from hydro-powered utilities via the Douglas County Public Utility District, very low energy rates (≈ $0.039 per kWh), and a dry, cool climate that reduces cooling costs.

Its location is seismically stable and proximate to major Pacific Northwest hubs including Seattle and Hillsboro, making it well-suited for disaster recovery, high-density computing workloads (e.g. GPU, AI), and enterprises seeking low total cost of ownership (TCO). 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Power Capacity & Efficiency: 70 MW of aggregate critical power across the campus. 
  • Annualized PUE: Approximately 1.2, reflecting high energy efficiency. 
  • Low Energy Cost: Power rates around $0.039/kWh, sourced from sustainable hydroelectric power.
  • Free Cooling & Climate Advantage: “Free cooling” (economizer / evaporative systems) used most of the year due to favorable climate; also options for liquid cooling / high-density racks. 
  • Modular Design & Scalability: Buildings with flexible floor plans; Building E under design/planning adding ~18 MW of capacity; large ceiling heights (21-22’ floor-to-ceiling; ~14’ to drop ceiling with ~8’ plenum) for infrastructure flexibility. 
  • Infrastructure Redundancy: Fully redundant substation, transformers, transmission lines; redundant electrical and mechanical systems; backup generators sized for emergency or maintenance; 72-hour onsite generators at peak load in some buildings. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical security: 24/7 onsite security personnel; perimeter security including setbacks, berms, fencing; secure access checkpoints; ballistic-rated building access points. 
  • Access control: Biometric locks on outside doors; mantraps at building entrances. 
  • Monitoring & operations: Continuous critical environment monitoring; onsite facilities staff 24/7; robust control and monitoring systems; fire detection systems above and below racks. 
  • Compliance / Certifications: The facility supports standard industry compliance certifications (HIPAA, HITECH, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, SOC 1 Type 2, SOC 2 Type 2, SSAE 18) according to third-party data sources. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral status: Multiple campus fiber entries and redundant pathways; dedicated Meet-Me-Rooms. 
  • Major carrier options include AT&T, Douglas County PUD, Lumen, Spectrum/Charter, Verizon, Zayo, Ziply Fiber, among others. 
  • Interconnection & fiber diversity: Dual campus fiber entries with redundant fiber paths; strong local and long-haul network reach.
  • Cloud on-ramps and Internet Exchange access: While specific IX points aren’t publicly detailed, the variety of Tier-1 networks, Meet-Me-Rooms, and carrier neutrality suggest robust interconnect and cloud-provider on-ramps. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises requiring cost-efficient, sustainable power (e.g. AI/ML workloads, GPU farms) that benefit from low PUE and hydroelectric sourcing. 
  • Organizations needing geographic resilience / disaster recovery outside seismic zones, yet close to major hubs (Seattle, Pacific Northwest). 
  • Hyperscalers and cloud providers seeking scalable, high-density infrastructure with flexible cooling solutions. 
  • Regulated industries (healthcare, finance, government) that require strong compliance, security, and reliability.
  • Firms for whom total cost of ownership (power, cooling, connectivity) is a major concern.
full_address: 4405 Grant Rd, East Wenatchee, WA 98802, USA

name: ORD01-2 Data Center

  • Power Capacity: ORD01-2 offers up to 72 MW of critical capacity. 
  • Scalable Design: Can be subdivided into two separate facilities. 
  • Cooling Systems: Uses Edged’s advanced waterless cooling technology via its ThermalWorks closed-loop system. Supports densities up to 70 kW/rack with air cooling and 200 kW/rack with plug-and-play liquid cooling.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical security includes 24×7×365 access control, perimeter fencing, gated entry. 
  • Multiple Meet-Me rooms and two loading dock bays per facility help with controlled access. 
  • Smoke detection systems: addressable detectors with early detection in hot aisles. Fire suppression using double-interlock pre-action mist system. 
  • On-site monitoring and secure DCIM platform covering all power and mechanical systems. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with multiple fiber providers. 
  • Four diverse points of fiber entry for redundancy. 
  • Meet-Me rooms: provides interconnection options within the facility.
  • Strong regional connectivity via Chicago’s role as a hub for long-haul fiber networks. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Large enterprise customers requiring high power and scalable infrastructure (AI / ML workloads, HPC, hyperscalers). 
  • Cloud and edge providers needing low latency across the Midwest and national backbone access. 
  • Businesses and institutions seeking sustainability-focused infrastructure (low water usage, high energy efficiency).

name: Chicago data center
description:
The CME Group Chicago Data Center (including its key hub in Aurora, Illinois) serves as the core infrastructure for the world-leading derivatives exchange operator. Located in the Chicago region with direct links into its trading venues, this facility is designed for ultra-low latency and high availability, enabling high-speed electronic trading on the CME Globex platform. 

 What makes this data centre stand out is its co-location ecosystem for trading firms, direct connectivity to the exchange’s engines, and a recent enhancement in partnership with Google Cloud to build a dedicated private cloud region adjacent to the facility.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • The primary Chicago/Aurora location (2905 Diehl Rd, Aurora IL) spans ~428,000 sq ft and supports up to ~92 MW redundant power (184 MW gross potential) via two separate 138 kV transmission feeders and a privately-owned substation. 
  • Designed on a Tier-III style architecture: concurrently maintainable power and cooling infrastructure suited for mission-critical trading operations. 
  • Cooling includes large-scale chillers, free-cooling capability in colder months, variable-frequency drives, and high-density rack support (e.g., 30-ton CRAC units were specified in one build).
  • Scalability: Built to support trading operations of CME and third-party clients (colocation, dedicated servers, cloud-on-ramp). Its size and power provisioning give headroom for growth within financial-services demand.
  • Support services: The facility offers co-location services, cross-connects, direct access to the Globex engine, and options for self-managed infrastructure or cloud/hybrid deployment via the Google Cloud partnership. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7 operations with high-security physical protections: multi-layer access control, cages for clients, and direct connectivity into CME’s trading infrastructure. The colocation guide describes multiple diverse fibre entrances and carrier-neutral telecom rooms. 
  • Built for high-availability: dual-feed power, generator backup systems, robust cooling redundancy, and dedicated monitoring aligned to financial-market uptime requirements.
  • Compliance: While specific certification details for this site are less publicly enumerated, the infrastructure supports the needs of regulated trading firms, with performance, security and connectivity consistent with 24/7 global markets.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral ecosystem with extensive cross-connect availability: Clients deploying in the centre gain direct access to the CME Globex platform, market data feeds and exchange infrastructure. 
  • Redundant fibre paths, low-latency network design and immediate access to the exchange engine deliver major advantages to trading firms operating time-sensitive workloads.
  • Cloud on-ramps and hybrid options: The partnership with Google Cloud establishes a dedicated private cloud region and colocation facility adjacent to the data centre, enabling clients to choose self-managed hardware or cloud services with equivalent latency into the exchange. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Trading firms, hedge funds, market-makers and algorithmic trading operators requiring ultra-low latency access to the CME Globex derivatives venue.
  • Financial institutions needing high availability infrastructure and direct access to exchange match-engines.
  • Technology, cloud and managed-service providers seeking colocation in the heart of the U.S. derivatives market with direct connectivity into global hubs.
  • Enterprises with latency-sensitive workloads, high computing demands or hybrid/edge-cloud architectures aligned to financial-market infrastructure.

name: DC3 DATA CENTER
description:
The DC3 data-centre facility operated by CME Group in Aurora, Illinois (approximately 35 miles from downtown Chicago) serves as a flagship trading infrastructure hub.  Designed specifically for ultra-low latency and high availability, the facility hosts the CME Globex matching engine and offers colocation and connectivity services tailored for the finance and trading industry. What makes this facility stand out is its large footprint (roughly 428,000 sq ft) and massive redundant power infrastructure which supports mission-critical trading operations and client deployments alike. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • The facility is built to a Tier III-design (or equivalent concurrently-maintainable standard) with infrastructure enhancements tailored to financial-markets workloads. 
  • Power & capacity: The campus supports up to 92 MW of redundant power (184 MW total capacity) with two separate 138 kV utility transmission services. The building footprint is ~428,000 sq ft.
  • Scalability & colocation: Clients can choose licensed half- or full-cabinets, dedicated cages or private suites. The power per cabinet is offered in tiers: 4.25 kW, 8.5 kW or 17 kW. 
  • Support services: The facility features 24/7 on-site remote-hands technicians, shipping/receiving services, customer support portal and full colocation operations. 
  • Unique features: This data centre is part of the core trading infrastructure of CME, offering equidistant cross-connects to the Globex engine, and is optimized for sub-millisecond latency trading environments. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical security: Multi-layer access control including biometric or card access, multi-zone security perimeters, full CCTV and man-trap style entries. While specific access lists are proprietary, the facility is described as “seven layers of physical security”. 
  • Infrastructure protection: Redundant power systems, multiple carrier rooms, redundant network entry points and reinforced building structure to support continuous trading operations.
  • Operational compliance: Though specific certifications are less publicised, the facility supports regulated financial market operations, with connectivity and support structured for exchange clients and trading firms.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral: The facility offers an open access telecommunications policy – multiple carriers, cross-connects and client choice are supported. 
  • Trading-grade connectivity: The “CME GLink” offers equidistant low-latency 10 Gbps links into the Globex engine, along with high-speed fibre cross-connects to other clients and carriers.
  • Low-latency network paths: Located in the Chicago region, the facility has connectivity pathways optimized to key trading hubs, reducing round-trip latency for market participants. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Financial institutions and proprietary trading firms requiring the lowest possible latency to futures/derivatives markets.
  • Exchanges, algorithmic traders and market-data providers who demand high power density, high availability and premium connectivity.
  • Service providers offering colocation, managed hosting or cloud/edge compute for trading or data-intensive workloads.
  • Any organisation where access to the exchange matching engine and market data sources gives a competitive edge.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Power Capacity: Approximately 4.5 MW of power capacity is available. 
  • Raised‐floor Technical / Colocation Space: The facility includes raised-floor space for efficient airflow and cable management; anti-static floors are used for equipment protection.
  • Redundancy & Cooling: Cooling infrastructure operates with N+1 redundancy; economization (air-side or water-side) is employed to improve energy efficiency. 
  • Building Size & Architecture: The larger building (“The Marq”) is ~ 522,656 sqft with up to 37,000 sqft of technical/data center space available in parts. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical & Access Controls: There are 24/7 security staff; controlled access via key-card / biometric / similar authentication; secure loading docks; monitoring with CCTV. 
  • Environmental & Fire Safety: Fire suppression (pre-action systems, dry pipe sprinklers, possibly gas suppression in certain provider-spaces); raised floors; humidity/temperature monitoring; water leak monitoring in cooling systems. 
  • Compliance Certifications: XO Communications facility in this building is audited under SSAE 16 / SOC-3; supports compliance standards including HIPAA, PCI, SOX 404. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-Neutral / Multiple Providers: The building hosts many carriers: Bell Canada, XO, Cogent, Lumen, AT&T, Verizon, Comcast, among others. Carrier-neutral access is available to clients. 
  • Fiber Entry & Network Diversity: Multiple fiber providers are present in building; built for diverse fiber paths. 
  • Internet Exchange & Interconnection Options: Supports dark fiber, lit services; in-building interconnection, cross-connects; strong IP backbone access via Cogent, XO, Bell, etc. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises needing high-availability colocation for critical infrastructure.
  • Carriers and network service providers who require carrier hotel / interconnection functions.
  • Companies subject to regulatory or compliance mandates (healthcare, finance, legal) needing SSAE 16/SOC, PCI, HIPAA compliance. 
  • Cloud providers, managed service providers, content delivery networks that need dense connectivity and proximity to a rich fiber and carrier ecosystem.
  • Organizations with disaster recovery, backup, or hybrid cloud strategies in the Upper Midwest seeking low-latency access, redundancy and diverse network paths.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • First building (Building A) is ~ 430,000 square feet, completed as of October 2024. 
  • Designed for up to 84 MW of critical IT power capacity at full campus build-out. 
  • Supports up to 200 kW per rack, enabling very high-density computing and liquid cooling environments. 
  • Constructed with modular/flexible data halls, with generous floor-to-ceiling height (approx. 21-22 ft), drop ceiling with ~14 ft plus an ~8 ft plenum overhead, allowing flexible configurations. 
  • Redundant mechanical and electrical systems; Building A has ~30 MW critical capacity, Building B planned for additional ~54 MW. 
  • Cooling systems include liquid cooling readiness, efficient air-cooling, indirect airside economizers, static heat exchangers; focus on water-free cooling where possible. 
  • On-site power supply backed by a substantial substation (on-site 200 MW substation referenced for campus feed) with diverse feeds. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access
  • Carrier-neutral facility.

  • On-site carrier-class Meet-Me rooms.
  • Multiple network providers / Tier-1 carriers supported (including AT&T, Charter, Cogent, Zayo, Logix etc.). 

💼 Who It Serves

name: Denver Data Center
description:
H5 Data Centers' Denver data center is a purpose-built facility, initially constructed by United Airlines and most recently owned by the global distribution system manager Galileo (a division of Travelport), a leading global travel commerce platform. The data center campus sits on more than 12 acres in the Denver Tech Center and is comprised of four buildings totaling nearly 300,000 square feet. H5 Data Centers' Denver data center campus actually supports two independent Tier III, concurrently maintainable data centers.

Since acquiring the data center, H5 Data Centers has invested nearly $20 million in mechanical and electrical upgrades, energy-efficiency improvements and physical data center build-outs. The Denver team is comprised of nearly 20 people who operate one of Colorado's best data centers and have an average tenure of over 15 years at the site. No other data center comes close to this average tenure. As a result, the team has succeeded to maintain 100% uptime at the Denver data center for nearly 15 years.

Denver's only wholesale data center accommodates the mission critical needs of multiple Fortune 500 companies and offers a unique combination of size, physical security, certifications, Fortune 500 customer base, access to carriers, and uptime track record.

Unique aspects of the Denver data center campus include:
  • 300,000 SF data center campus
  • Only true wholesale data center offering in Denver
  • 24x7 on-site engineering team, 24x7 on-site security team
  • Available office, staging, storage and conference room space
  • Ample, on-site parking
  • 18-wheeler secure loading dock access
  • Denver Tech Center location
  • Fortune 500 customer base
  • Not located adjacent to an airport and located away from any airport flight paths
  • Not located adjacent to a railway and freeway
Power

Xcel Energy provides the Denver data center with over 17.5 MWs of reliable and affordable power (average annual costs per kWh of less than $0.075) from diverse substations. H5 Data Centers in turn manages two independent concurrently maintainable Tier III data centers on the campus, each with separate mechanical and electrical systems. All systems are meticulously maintained by our 24x7 on-site Denver engineering team.

H5 Data Centers manages an on-site 400 kW rooftop solar power array which adds additional renewable power to the existing Xcel Energy energy mix but also reduces the peak demand quantity (which reduces overall energy costs).

Electrical and mechanical highlights include:
  • Diverse utility feeds from Xcel Energy
  • N+1 or 2N+1 UPS distribution options
  • Two (2) concurrently maintainable data centers
  • Two (2) independent N+1 emergency generator lineups
  • Multiple underground diesel fuel storage tanks
  • On-site, 400 kW rooftop solar power array
  • Less than $0.075/kwh avg. annual power pricing from Xcel Energy
Security

H5 Data Centers' Denver data center campus is one of the country's most secure data center site's. The sites best-in-class physical security include motion-sensitive fences, 24x7 on-site security staff, and multiple layers of dual-authentication access checkpoints. After the events of 9/11, the United States Department of Transportation (DOT) even approved of the data center's security parameters.

The data center offers some of the following security benefits:
  • Mantraps
  • Biometric and key card access controls
  • Motion-detection fences
  • 100+ on-site cameras monitored 24x7
  • Customers that include some of the world's leading defense contractors
  • Annual Type 2 SOC 1 audits
  • Annual ISO 27001 certification
Network

H5 Data Centers' Denver data center offers carrier-neutral colocation and data center services, providing customers with access to a variety of different regional, national and international telecommunications carriers and fiber providers. With its carrier partners, H5 Data Centers serves a growing community of enterprises, content delivery networks (CDNs) and cloud service providers seeking to improve their mission critical operations.

There are two (2) independent meet-me rooms on the Denver data center campus that are backed by separate mechanical and electrical infrastructure.

Highlights of H5 Data Centers' Denver network-neutral data center:
  • Access to nearly 10 national and regional carriers
  • Diverse fiber points of entry
  • Two (2) independent meet-me rooms
  • Cross connections available within 2 business days of order
Features

Denver ranks as one of the safest locations to place a data center in the United States, as it is very unlikely to be affected by tornadoes, earthquakes, hurricanes, or flooding. Further, H5 Data Center's Denver data center offers easy access to Denver International Airport.

H5 Data Centers conducts annual Type 2 SOC 1 audits and ISO 27001 certification.

H5 Data Centers' Denver data center product offering includes:
  • Turn-key wholesale data center space
  • Customized cages and private suites
  • Powered shell build-outs
  • Office and storage space
  • 24x7 remote hands services

The RCH‑1 Iron Mountain Data Center is the first phase of a new, hyperscale-capable data center campus being developed by Iron Mountain Data Centers at White Oak Technology Park, in Henrico County, Richmond, Virginia. Positioned east of Richmond along major corridors I‑64 and I‑95—and near subsea fiber cable landings in Virginia Beach—this site offers strategic connectivity advantages and proximity to “Data Center Alley.” It serves as a prime alternative to Northern Virginia’s crowded data center market.

The campus spans 66 acres, with RCH‑1 comprising a 364,860 ft² facility designed to deliver up to 48 MW of capacity. The initial 18 MW of capacity is slated for availability by 2027, while full build-out of RCH‑1 is expected by 2030, with the remainder of the campus delivering over 200 MW of total capacity.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Campus & Facility Scale: 66‑acre site; RCH‑1 offers 364,860 ft² (~34,000 m²) with 48 MW total capacity at full build-out.
  • Phased Roll-Out: 18 MW expected by 2027; full 48 MW complete by 2030.
  • Scalable Infrastructure: Part of a campus designed to exceed 200 MW, supporting hyperscale growth.
  • Renewable Power & Efficiency: Campus committed to 100% renewable energy and built to high energy efficiency standards.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Rigorous Compliance Standards: Planned compliance certifications for the Richmond campus include HIPAA, FISMA High, PCI-DSS, ISO 27001, ISO 50001, SOC 2/3, among others.
  • Iron Mountain’s Trusted Security Legacy: Leveraging the company’s longstanding reputation for secure, highly regulated facilities.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Exceptional Network Access: The campus sits near major long-haul fiber providers, I‑64 / I‑95 corridors, and subsea cable landings in Virginia Beach—enabling robust, diverse connectivity.
  • Carrier‑Neutral Ecosystem: Iron Mountain affords cloud-neutral, carrier-neutral interconnection, offering access to hyperscale clouds, CDNs, tier‑1 networks, peering and exchange services.

💼 Who It Serves

  • Hyperscale Customers: Designed to support large-scale cloud providers and computing-intensive data operations.
  • Highly Regulated Organizations: Ideal for industries like government, healthcare, and financial services requiring stringent compliance support.
  • Enterprises Seeking Virginia-Based Alternatives: Attracts businesses looking for colocation options with lower congestion and competitive incentives relative to Northern Virginia.

name: 200 Paul Data Center
description:
The 200 Paul Data Center, operated by UnitedLayer, is located at 200 Paul Avenue, San Francisco, CA, USA.  This facility is one of San Francisco’s premier colocation hubs, built on solid bedrock and designed with seismic and flood resilience in mind (outside floodplain, seismic Zone 4) to ensure continuity even in adverse geologic or weather events. 

As a Tier-III / Tier-3+ equivalent facility with robust connectivity, it serves as a major interconnection point on the U.S. West Coast, providing enterprises, cloud providers, and network operators with a highly reliable data center presence in the Bay Area. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Tier-III infrastructure with N+1 architecture for both power and cooling systems 
  • Cooling redundancy: N+1 configuration with multiple CRAC units and cold-aisle containment layout 
  • Power backup: redundant diesel generators with N+1 redundancy, multiple UPS units, and A/B feed design via PDUs 
  • High power density support: the facility supports high density loads (e.g. ~150 W/ft² and up to 10 kW per cabinet in many layouts) 
  • Raised floor / airflow design: raised-floor colocation space (~32,000 sq ft) to support efficient cabling, airflow, and modular deployment
  • Large building footprint: ~512,642 sq ft total facility area 
  • Seismic & structural design: built on bedrock with Zone 4 rating, and constructed outside the 500-year floodplain for enhanced resilience
  • Multi-cloud connectivity: direct connections to major public cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) over fully redundant 10G infrastructure

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7 onsite security with personnel, CCTV, motion detectors, and video surveillance with recording 
  • Access control: card access + biometric scanning, mantrap entry to critical zones
  • Certifications & compliance: SSAE-18 / SSAE-16, SOC 1, SOC 2, PCI DSS, HIPAA, FedRAMP, ISO 27001, ISO 9001, ISO 14001, NIST 800-53, LEED 
  • Independently audited: regular audits conform to industry standards for security, availability, and controls 
  • Monitoring & control: environment and infrastructure monitored continuously (temperature, humidity, power, etc.) to detect anomalies early 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with cross-connect and meet-me-room flexibility 
  • Dense carrier ecosystem: over 60 carriers on-site, and direct access to thousands of carriers via broader network infrastructure
  • Redundant fiber entry and path diversity to maintain resilience in connectivity 
  • Redundant 10 Gb infrastructure, with transit and transport options up to 100 Gbps in many configurations
  • Layer-2 connectivity to multiple buildings and facilities in San Francisco, supporting DCI and inter-building networking 

. 💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises and cloud / SaaS providers needing a reliable Bay Area / West Coast presence
  • Financial services, tech firms, media, or content delivery networks seeking high density, low latency infrastructure
  • Regulated industries requiring strict compliance (PCI, HIPAA, FedRAMP)
  • Hybrid cloud users wanting direct access and integration to AWS, Azure, GCP
  • Organizations demanding resilient infrastructure (seismic, flood-resistant, high availability) in a mission-critical metro

name: PIT2 - Summit Park Data Center
description:
Welcome to Pittsburgh, a city known for its history as a port and its ability to connect markets. But now, Pittsburgh has transformed into so much more. It has become a world-class hub for technology, research, and education. The heart of this transformation lies in the Pittsburgh Innovation District, where creativity and innovation thrive. In this district, you'll find prestigious institutions such as the University of Pittsburgh and Carnegie Mellon University, which is home to the renowned Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center. Additionally, the district is proud to host UPMC.

But it's not just about innovation and education in Pittsburgh. This city also offers a low risk of natural disasters compared to the national average, making it a safe and dependable location. Not only that, but commercial power is cost-effective, making it an attractive choice for businesses.

When it comes to data services, DataBank is here to serve. In Pittsburgh, we offer two options: PIT1, our downtown data center, and PIT2, located in Summit Park. Both facilities provide top-notch power, cooling, connectivity, and security controls. PIT2, in particular, is an impressive site. It anchors our Summit Park Campus, boasting 17 acres of space and the potential for two additional data centers. This facility, conveniently located only 15 minutes from the airport, is positioned right across the street from a utility-owned substation. Recent improvements have made it the premier data center for scalable workloads in the metro area.

In summary, Pittsburgh is no longer just a port city; it's a city of innovation and opportunity. With its strong infrastructure, low risk, and DataBank's exceptional data centers, it's the ideal place for businesses to grow and thrive.

name: T-Mobile 50 NE 9th St
description:
401 North Broad Street (“401 N Broad”) is a major carrier-neutral interconnection and colocation data center facility located in Center City Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. It occupies the historic Terminal Commerce Building, a large Art Deco historic complex with over 1.3 million gross square feet. Owned and operated by Netrality Properties, 401 N Broad is one of the most fiber-dense and network-rich facilities on the U.S. Northeast corridor between New York and Virginia. Its positioning in Center City provides strategic advantages: dense metro and long-haul fiber routes, proximity to major business, financial, healthcare, and academic institutions, and easy access for on-site staffing and cross-connects. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Total Building Size: ~ 1,300,000 sq ft in the Terminal Commerce structure. 
  • Power Infrastructure: Utility feed capacity about 40,000 kW, backup generator power ~ 4,000 kW N+1, UPS system rated at ~ 1,200 kVA with 2N redundancy. 
  • Cooling Systems: Condenser water systems (~3,600 tons capacity) in N+1 redundancy, HVAC/data room CRAC units, cold-aisle containment. 
  • Structural / Physical Features: Floor loading capacity ~ 200 lb/sq ft (center to center), ceiling heights slab-to-slab ~ 12 ft in many areas, overhead cable ladder racks, secure loading docks, freight elevators (capacity ~ 10,000 lbs). Building is outside the 100-year floodplain. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical Security Measures: On-site 24/7 network operations center (NOC), keycard access, biometric access, mantraps, secure multi-bay loading dock, CCTV surveillance systems.
  • Monitoring & Staffing: 24/7 staffed operations; critical facility monitoring system. 
  • Compliance Certifications: The facility is compliant with HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC Audit standards (e.g. SSAE-16 / SOC 1, SOC 2) according to multiple listings. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-Neutral Status: Yes. Netrality’s 401 N Broad is a major carrier hotel / interconnection hub. 
  • On-Site Carriers / Providers: Over 70 unique network providers are on-net. Tenants include Zayo, Lumen, Crown Castle, XO (among others). 
  • Fiber Entry / Diversity: Six diverse Points of Entry (POEs) for fiber into the building; pre-built innerducts from POEs to the Meet-Me Room; multiple risers. 
  • Meet-Me Room (MMR): Yes, the facility includes a Meet-Me Room for tenant/provider interconnection. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises in finance, healthcare, education, and life sciences seeking highly interconnected, secure, and regulated data infrastructure in the mid-Atlantic / Northeast corridor.
  • Network operators, cloud providers, content delivery networks (CDNs), managed service providers (MSPs), and telecom carriers who need rich fiber density, carrier neutrality, and numerous interconnection options.
  • Organizations requiring compliance certifications (e.g. HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC) and high availability infrastructure for critical workloads.

description:
The CHI1 – Chicago Data Center (ColoCrossing), located at 2200 Busse Road, Elk Grove Village, Illinois, serves as a major colocation and interconnection hub just outside Chicago.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Electrical capacity: 36.4 MW feed 
  • Generator setup: 32 diesel power units, each ~2.25 MW 
  • UPS systems: 16 rotary UPS units each ~1,300 kW 
  • Cooling: 16 centrifugal chillers at ~1,150 ton each; 1,000,000 gallon chilled water storage for ~24 minutes full load operation 
  • Floor load: ~450 lb/ft² 
  • Power density: supports ~10 kW per cabinet (high-density capable)
  • Redundancy: minimum N+1 on major systems (cooling, power) 
  • Diverse fiber entry: two fiber vaults / entry paths for redundancy 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Building & perimeter protection: bullet-resistant walls/doors at check-in, level III rated in sections
  • 24/7 staffed security (minimum two security officers) 
  • Access control: dual-factor authentication (badge + biometrics
  • CCTV / video surveillance with archiving
  • Environmental monitoring (temperature, humidity, etc.)
  • Fire suppression & detection: very early smoke detection (VESDA), dry-pipe / pre-action systems
  • Support services: 24/7 remote hands and on-site technical support 
  • Compliance/certifications: SSAE 16 (Type I) historically cited for the site 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral with meet-me room for cross-connects 
  • Backbone connections: ColoCrossing network with capacity and path diversity 
  • Redundant fiber entry / paths through different vaults 
  • Network capacity: ~80 Gbps routing capacity (as cited) 
  • Extensive carrier presence onsite (multiple network providers) 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises requiring high-availability colocation in the Chicago metro corridor
  • Cloud providers, SaaS firms, and platform vendors needing dense interconnectivity
  • Networking operators, ISPs, and content delivery providers seeking cross-connect and backbone access

Construction of the second phase is currently under way and will add an additional twelve Turn-Key Flex Data Centers, bringing the total to 22. The Turn-Key Flex Suites are designed with cooling technology that allows for the use of outside air economization to increase efficiency and decrease PUE. The facility is situated on a secure campus protected by perimeter fencing, manned guard gates, berms and hard-scaping. Each building has a single, main entry point protected by card key access and a 24x7x365 guard station. Additional security is provided by CCTV and biometric access controls. HVAC, Power and building critical systems are managed and monitored 24x7x365 by highly trained Digital Realty employees. The Ashburn campus is powered by 80 MW from the Greenway Substation which is located on Digital Realty’s property. The construction of a second dedicated substation, the Enterprise Substation, also located on Digital Realty’s property was constructed and completed in early 2014. This brings the total capacity of the Digital Ashburn campus to 230MW. Power is distributed to each building via dedicated underground, concrete encased duct banks. The power is provided by one of the most reliable transmission systems in the country with reduced risk of electrical interruptions and rates that are below the national average.

name: Hillsboro HI1 Data Center
description:
The Hillsboro HI1 Data Center is operated by NTT Global Data Centers Americas, formerly known as RagingWire, and is situated at 4050 NE Evergreen Road, Hillsboro, Oregon 97124, USA. As the inaugural facility of a five-building campus with a total planned capacity of 126 MW, it benefits from its location in the Pacific Northwest's technology hub, offering direct access to advanced subsea connectivity through NTT’s Subsea Connect network.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Total Power Capacity: Delivers 36 MW of critical IT load, divided across six modular 6 MW vaults, each with N+1 distributed redundancy.
  • Engineering Design: Features dual 34.5 kV feeders, each rated at 60 MVA, with eight 1 MW UPS modules per vault and dual-corded rack power distribution.
  • Cooling Systems: Employs air-cooled chillers with airside economizers, fan coil wall systems, dedicated modular chiller plants, sealed-loop water cooling, hot aisle containment, and N+1 redundancy.
  • Colocation Options: Offers a variety of deployment models including cabinets, customizable cages, private suites, and dedicated vaults.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Security Infrastructure: Protected by 24/7 on-site operations and security teams, multi-factor authentication, over 100 real-time security feeds, and a fenced campus with centrally controlled access.
  • Compliance Certifications: Holds a robust compliance profile including SOC 1 & 2 Type II, ISO 27001, ISO 50001, PCI DSS, NIST 800‑53 High, HIPAA, and Cleaner Air Oregoncertification.
  • Sustainability: Designed to LEED Gold standards and recognized with EPA ENERGY STARcertification.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-Neutral: Provides three diverse fiber entry points, two Meet-Me Rooms, and support for a dense ecosystem including public, private, and hybrid cloud providers.
  • Subsea Connect: Features NTT’s dedicatedSubsea Connect service, offering secure, low-latency transpacific connectivity to Asia-Pacific via dedicated 10G/100G lit fiber.

💼 Who It Serves

The Hillsboro HI1 facility is ideal for:

  • Cloud providers, hyperscalers, content networks, and enterprises needing scalable, secure colocation in a green-compliant, low-cost Oregon environment.
  • Use cases requiring high-density deployments (up to 22 kW/rack), backed by SLA-grade reliability and energy-efficient design.
  • Organizations focused on global interconnection, leveraging NTT’s transpacific infrastructure for Asia access.

description:
Stream Data Centers’ DFW VII facility, located at 1813 Lookout Drive, Garland, Texas, sits on a 23‑acre site (or in some sources, 22.6 acre) in the northeast Dallas / Garland suburb—positioned to serve booming metro‑Dallas connectivity and power infrastructure. Operator Stream Data Centers delivers hyperscale and wholesale colocation services, particularly to cloud providers, large enterprises, and Fortune‑500 organizations seeking high-density, low-latency, and resilient infrastructure.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Site & Building: Free‑standing, Tier III‑designed structure, wind‑rated for up to 135 mph, on 23 acres 
  • Size: Initial 138,132 sq ft data center with expansion to 275,000 sq ft (and eventual campus up to 400,000 sq ft)
  • Power Capacity: Designed to support 43 MW of 2N critical load, with capability to scale; initial building: 15 MW for first phase 
  • Utility Power: Dual dedicated underground Oncor utility feeds—two 40 MW feeders from on‑site substation, collectively enabling up to 43 MW capacity; feeders are 100% dedicated to Stream’s campus 
  • Backup Power: On‑site fuel storage providing 48 hours of backup power 
  • Cooling Infrastructure: 36‑inch raised floor over depressed slab; N+1 high-efficiency air‑cooled chillers; bi‑directional chilled‑water loop and real‑time temperature/humidity sensors; chilled‑water available for in‑row/in‑rack cooling solutions 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Security Measures: 100‑foot setbacks from property line to data halls; secured perimeter with a manned Perimeter Security Center (PSC)
  • Access Control & Monitoring: Two‑person minimum, around‑the‑clock guard force; interior Security Operations Center (SOC); extensive interior/exterior video surveillance and access control systems, with recorded data retained for at least 90 days

name: Los Angeles (LAX1-Hawthorne) Data Center
description:
Introducing Centersquare's Hawthorne facility, a state-of-the-art data center just 6 miles away from Los Angeles International Airport. With over 288,000 square feet of space, this facility is equipped with cutting-edge technology and amenities.

To prioritize sustainability, the center generates 1MW of power onsite through high-efficiency fuel cells. Additionally, a dedicated 28MVA substation ensures uninterrupted power supply, guaranteeing 100% uptime.

Here are some key features of the data center:

Total space: 288,583 sq. ft.

Colocation area: 132,398 sq. ft.

Office space: Customizable customer drop-in cubicles

Site area: Commercial

Seismic zone: 4

Built in 1962, retrofitted in 2001

The data center offers various types of storage solutions, including cages and locking cabinets. Standard racks are available, with a raised floor load of 150 lbs. per sq. ft. and a sub-floor load of 300 lbs. per sq. ft. Flexible cross-connect options, including copper, fiber, and coaxial, are available. Remote hands support is provided 24/7, and there is a loading dock for convenience.

Electricity capacity is ample, with 150 w/sq. ft. in each cabinet. The UPS configuration is 2N, and there is one utility feeder. The standby power configuration is N+1, provided by four 2,000 kW diesel generators and four 25,000-gallon underground fuel tanks.

For optimal cooling and temperature control, the data center has N+1 HVAC distribution and an N+1 HVAC system. Three 600-ton chillers with onsite make-up water ensure efficient cooling. Fire safety is a top priority, with VESDA smoke detection and a conventional addressable fire alarm system. Pre-action dry pipe fire suppression is implemented in data center areas, while wet sprinklers are used in non-equipment areas.

Experience top-notch data center services at Centersquare's Hawthorne facility – the perfect solution for all your IT needs.

Standard power to rack at 3.5kVA, with the option to scale up to 5kVA.

name: Hut 8 Vega
description:
Hut 8 Vega is a Hut 8 Corp. data center facility located in Oldham County, West Texas, near Vega, Texas, United States. Hut 8 announced the initial energization of Vega on June 30, 2025, describing it as a 205 MW facility designed for rack-based ASIC compute using direct-to-chip liquid cooling.

The site is positioned in the Texas Panhandle within the ERCOT power market, with access to both behind-the-meter wind generation and front-of-the-meter ERCOT grid power. Vega is primarily positioned for Bitcoin mining ASIC colocation, while Hut 8 also frames the architecture as relevant to future high-density HPC workloads.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  1. Tier I data center form factor
  2. 205 MW nameplate energy capacity
  3. 162,000 sq. ft. facility footprint
  4. Rack-based ASIC compute architecture
  5. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling system designed by Hut 8
  6. Supports ASIC deployments up to 180 kW per rack
  7. Modular systems include pump skids, fluid distribution networks, server racks, switchboards, and smart PDUs
  8. 96 custom cooling modules
  9. Closed-loop reverse-return cooling system circulating 120,000 gallons of glycol-water solution
  10. Designed to reduce water consumption versus conventional high-density cooling systems
  11. Power usage effectiveness reported as 1.06
  12. Powered behind-the-meter by a wind farm and front-of-the-meter by the ERCOT grid
  13. At full energization, designed to host up to 17,280 BITMAIN U3S21EXPH servers
  14. BITMAIN is the client for the full ~15 EH/s deployment under an ASIC colocation agreement

🔐 Security & Compliance

  1. Purpose-built facility for high-density ASIC compute infrastructure
  2. Controlled operational environment for hosted mining equipment
  3. Modular rack-based deployment architecture
  4. Customer deployment tied to BITMAIN ASIC colocation agreement
  5. Fire detection and suppression systems should be confirmed directly during diligence
  6. Certification claims should be verified directly with Hut 8 during procurement

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  1. Designed for energy-intensive compute rather than traditional enterprise carrier-dense colocation
  2. Supports hosted ASIC infrastructure under a colocation agreement
  3. Located in West Texas with energy access through behind-the-meter wind and ERCOT grid supply
  4. Suitable for Bitcoin mining, ASIC hosting, and select non-customer-facing high-density compute workloads
  5. Not presented publicly as an Internet exchange or enterprise carrier hotel

💼 Who It Serves

  1. ASIC colocation customers
  2. Bitcoin mining operators
  3. High-density compute infrastructure users
  4. Energy-intensive digital infrastructure deployments
  5. Organizations evaluating application-optimized compute sites
  6. Potential future HPC workloads where location and latency are less critical

name: 350 East Cermak Data Center
description:

Operated by Digital Realty Trust, it stands as one of the largest data centers in the world, spanning over 1.1 million square feet, and serves as a premier carrier hotel, offering unmatched connectivity in the Midwest. Strategically positioned minutes from McCormick Place, Chicago’s financial district, and both O’Hare and Midway airports, the facility benefits from exceptional accessibility and proximity to major transport and business hubs. ⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Gross Building Size: Approximately 1,133,000 sq ft over 8 levels 
  • Utility Power Capacity: ~109,000 kW (109 MW) 
  • UPS Power Capacity: ~70,000 kW, supporting N+1 and 2N redundancy
  • Generator Power Capacity: ~48,900 kW and noted in other sources and up to 150,000 kW
  • Power & Cooling Redundancy: Cooling systems with N+1 redundancy and in-room cooling redundancy of N+15%; heat rejection redundancy N+1 
  • Max Power Density: Ranges between 140–300 W/sq ft 
  • Load-Bearing Capacity: Roof uses membrane and reinforced concrete decking; floor loading rated up to 250 lbs/sq ft 
  • Fiber & Building Entry: Features multiple diverse fiber entrances through several fiber vaults

  • 24/7 on-site security, biometric access, man-traps, and extensive CCTV coverage are standard at this level of facility. (While specific public certification lists for this site are general, such features are referenced in spec materials.)

name: PHX-A1 Metrobloks Data Center

The facility is listed as coming soon and supports customizable configurations for both single-tenant and multi-tenant deployments. Metrobloks positions PHX-A1 for customers that need urban data center capacity close to the Phoenix carrier ecosystem, major hyperscaler availability zones, and dense regional demand.

The facility delivers 17.6 MW of total critical IT capacity, 127,000 square feet of whitespace, and flexible power densities from 5 kW to more than 150 kW per rack. PHX-A1 is built to Uptime Institute Tier III standards and combines hybrid air cooling with direct-to-chip liquid cooling for high-density compute. The site is adjacent to an APS 230 kV substation, has 23 MW redundant power supply, and supports dense thermal loads with air-based, liquid-based, or hybrid configurations.

PHX-A1 benefits from a downtown Phoenix location with proximity to Phoenix NAP and the 120 East Van Buren carrier hub. Metrobloks lists low-latency access to 10.1 million people within 20 milliseconds, proximity to multiple local hyperscaler availability zones, and state, county, and local Transaction Privilege Tax and Use Tax exemptions. The facility sits outside historic flood plains and is pre-zoned for telecom and data center use, giving PHX-A1 a strong position for AI, cloud, enterprise, and interconnection-oriented deployments in one of the largest U.S. data center markets.

name: Fredericksburg VA4-Building 1 Data Cente
description:
PNH4 Fredericksburg VA4-Building 1 Data Center, part of the VA4 Fredericksburg Data Center Campusoperated by Vantage Data Centers, is a next-generation hyperscale facility under development in Stafford County, near Fredericksburg, Virginia, United States. Situated approximately an hour south of Northern Virginia’s core data center cluster (“Data Center Alley”), the VA4 campus expands Vantage’s Virginia footprint to 782 MW of critical IT capacity and brings state-of-the-art infrastructure to a rapidly growing digital hub outside highly congested metro zones.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  1. Designed as part of an 82-acre campus with three data centers totalling ~929,000 sq ft and 192 MW of critical IT load.
  2. High-availability, enterprise-grade design supporting mission-critical and high-density compute, networking, and storage workloads.
  3. Redundant electrical systems with N+1 redundancy and a private 270 MW on-site substation.
  4. Flexible support for both traditional air-cooled infrastructure and next-generation GPU and liquid-cooled IT loads.
  5. Cooling architecture featuring a closed-loop chilled water system and optional liquid-to-liquid CDU-based designs capable of handling 100 % of IT loads.
  6. Modular layout with scalable colocation space and flexible room design options.
  7. Targeted phased completion, with Building 1 expected to be operational in late 2027.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  1. Multi-layer physical security with perimeter protection, 24/7 CCTV, and controlled access.
  2. Dual authentication using badge/PIN and biometric readers for customer and critical infrastructure zones.
  3. Visitor management and badging systems ensuring tracked onsite access.
  4. Fire detection and suppression systems designed to protect IT assets (industry standard).
  5. Compliance posture aligned with U.S. and industry best practices for secure data center operations.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  1. Carrier-neutral environment enabling interconnection with multiple network and cloud service providers.
  2. Multiple diverse fiber pathways into the campus with dark and lit fiber options.
  3. Two Meet-Me-Rooms (MMRs) per data center and dedicated points of entry for maximum path diversity.
  4. Designed for low-latency access across Virginia, including Northern Virginia, Washington D.C., and broader East Coast markets.

💼 Who It Serves

  1. Hyperscale cloud and AI service providers seeking high-capacity infrastructure.
  2. Large enterprises and technology platforms with high-performance and low-latency requirements.
  3. Telecom carriers, network operators, and managed service providers expanding fiber and interconnection footprints.
  4. Organizations deploying hybrid IT, edge, or multi-site architectures in the U.S. mid-Atlantic.

  1. Part of a state-of-the-art data center campus planned to host three two-story facilities with a combined 192 MW of critical IT load.

  4. Robust electrical infrastructure includes support from a private 270 MW on-site substation and dedicated utility power.
  5. Flexible cooling architecture supports both traditional air-cooled compute and advanced liquid-cooled systems capable of handling 100 % of IT loads.
  6. Modular facility configuration supporting scalable colocation, private suites, and customer-specific build-outs.
  7. Scheduled phased delivery, with the first building on campus expected to be operational in late 2027.

  1. Multi-layer physical security, including perimeter fencing, controlled access points, and 24/7 on-site security personnel.
  2. Continuous CCTV monitoring with coverage of all major access points and critical infrastructure areas.
  3. Dual authentication for secure access (badge/PIN plus biometric) to sensitive areas.
  4. Fire detection and suppression systems designed to protect IT environments and minimize risk.
  5. Built under Vantage’s global operational standards with alignment to major compliance frameworks and best practices, including U.S. data protection and physical security requirements.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  2. At least two Meet-Me-Rooms (MMRs) and diverse point-of-entry pathways per building for maximum connectivity redundancy.

💼 Who It Serves

Our facility is equipped with redundant UPS systems (2N) and boasts a maximum power density of 150 watts/ft2. We also have a generator power capacity of 12,000 kW to ensure uninterrupted operations.

But that's not all. Deft offers a fully redundant cooling plant, a cutting-edge IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack native and MPLS-enabled 10-GbE private global IP network for fast and secure connections, and low-latency peering with top domestic and international traffic destinations. We have two diverse fiber paths to Deft and Equinix IAD campus with 10/100G waves, direct links to all major carriers and public cloud providers, and even dark fiber availability.

We prioritize security to protect your assets. Our facility is monitored by 24/7 on-site security staff, with visitor screening and CCTV with 90-day backup. For additional layers of protection, we have dual-factor authentication via biometric and proximity sensors and equipment screening. To ensure physical security, we have crash-rated/anti-climb cages.

Don't settle for anything less when it comes to your data. Choose Deft's Ashburn facility for unparalleled reliability, security, and performance.

name: SDC New York
description:
Sabey Data Centers’ SDC Manhattan (also known as Intergate.Manhattan) is located at 375 Pearl Street, Lower Manhattan, NY — the former Verizon Building, now transformed into New York City’s only purpose-built data center facility. Operated by Sabey Data Centers, a privately owned multi-tenant data center developer & operator, this facility is strategically placed in the heart of NYC’s financial, media and business districts. Its location provides extremely low latency into metro and long-haul networks, with proximity to major business, financial, and enterprise hubs across the Tri-State region. 

Unique positioning factors include its vertical design in a high-rise building, substantial power and cooling capacity, carrier neutrality, infrastructure built to withstand environmental risk (such as flood / high-water concerns), and its ability to offer powered shell, build-to-suit, and colocation services in flexible, high-density environments. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Approx 18 MW capacity, with plans / support to scale up toward 40 MW as demand and build-out proceed. 
  • Over 1,000,000 square feet of total space in the building, with ~222,000 sq ft dedicated footprint for data center operations. 
  • Floors built for data center use: multiple floors (e.g. 6, 7, 11, 12) are fitted with data halls / technical space. Mechanical and electrical infrastructure on intermediate floors. 
  • Modular colocation spaces, powered shell, and build-to-suit deployment options. 
  • The facility is built with independent redundant mechanical systems per deployment / module. 
  • Generators rated to sustain about 72 hours runtime at peak load with on-site fuel supply, plus supplemental fuel contracts. 
  • Cooling infrastructure: multiple large chillers, cooling towers, hot-aisle containment, and water-cooled or economizer/free-cooling options when outside air conditions permit. 
  • Substations and electrical feeds are positioned at least ~46 feet above sea level for disaster resilience. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • On-site, skilled operations & engineering teams working 24/7 to maintain uptime and manage remote hands / dispatch as needed. 
  • Multiple access control layers with surveillance and monitoring systems across the facility. 
  • Fire detection systems installed both above and below racks. 
  • Compliance: the facility is declared to adhere to multiple standards in its compliance program. (While I did not find a precise list of certificates like ISO/Tier used in all cases, Baxtel lists it as “HIPAA certified, Tier-3 equivalent” in one listing. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral facility with direct access to many carriers. Some named carriers in the building include AT&T, Axiom Fiber, Cogent, EarthLink, GTT, Level 3, Lightpath, Lightower, OCG, Spectrum, United Fiber & Data, Verizon, XO, Zayo, ZenFi.
  • Diverse fiber entry paths: north and south risers along the building, manhole to Meet-Me Rooms, multiple carrier pathways. 
  • Access to Sabey’s own private communications/Intergate.Exchange network. 
  • Strong interconnection opportunities: rich telecom hub environment given its former Verizon switching building heritage. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Financial services firms located in Manhattan or needing very low latency to Wall Street and other finance/markets operations. 
  • Enterprises and cloud providers needing colocation or build-to-suit deployments in a high-density, secure environment.
  • Media, tech, and digital content companies requiring strong connectivity to carriers and network infrastructure.
  • Any business with mission-critical operations that demands high resilience, redundancy, and compliant infrastructure in NYC.
