name: One Wilshire Data Center
description:
The One Wilshire Data Center, located at 624 South Grand Avenue, Los Angeles, CA, is one of the world’s most densely connected carrier hotels. Operated and leased by CoreSpace (and underlying infrastructure managed by CoreSite’s LA1 facility), it serves as a strategic interconnection hub for West Coast, trans-Pacific, and global traffic. 

Because this site houses the One Wilshire Meet-Me Room, it is central to network peering, cloud connectivity, and carrier aggregation.Its location in downtown Los Angeles provides proximity to enterprise, media, entertainment, and regional network infrastructure, enabling low-latency access and robust connectivity. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Colocation / interconnection space: ~173,000 sq ft of data center space (across multiple floors) 
  • Structural specs: clear height ~12 ft 8.5 in above solid floor in many areas 
  • Cooling: condenser water cooling, N+1 cooling towers and pumps, chiller plant with redundancy 
  • Power & reliability:
  • • AC and DC power availability 
  • • UPS / PDU / RPP redundancy: N+1 
  • • Generators: N+1 redundancy; on-site fuel storage (~24 h) 
  • • Diverse utility feeds, multiple POEs, diverse MDF/IDF paths
  • Connectivity capacity: dark fiber tether to CoreSite LA2, enabling campus cross-connects 
  • Interconnection / Cloud access: access to CoreSite’s Open Cloud Exchange, AWS Direct Connect, Azure ExpressRoute, BBIX, Any2Exchange
  • Structural resilience: building is located above the 500-year flood plain per datasheet 
  • Operations & remote support: 24×7 remote hands & field ops personnel 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Access control: key cards, biometric scanners, double mantrap entries 
  • Surveillance: perimeter and interior IP-DVR / CCTV cameras 
  • Security staff: 24×7 security personnel with continuous monitoring 
  • Fire detection & suppression: dual interlock pre-action dry-pipe sprinkler systems, VESDA early smoke detection 
  • Certifications & compliance: SOC 1 Type 2, SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI DSS, HIPAA 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral: facility supports cross-connects across fiber, copper, coax 
  • Meet-Me Room: One of the densest carrier interconnect hubs in the Western U.S., hosting over 250 service providers 
  • Subsea / global reach: multiple submarine cable terminations and trans-Pacific connectivity pass through One Wilshire building 
  • Fiber diversity: multiple POEs, diverse fiber entry paths, dark fiber routes to LA2 and other nodes
  • Cloud / peering interconnect: Any2Exchange for Internet peering, Open Cloud Exchange, AWS Direct Connect, GCP, Azure ExpressRoute, BBIX 
  • Cross-connect types: fiber, copper, coaxial links available 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Network operators, ISPs, and carriers seeking dense interconnection and peering at a central hub
  • Content delivery networks (CDNs), media & entertainment firms delivering globally
  • Cloud and IT service providers requiring hybrid cloud connectivity and low-latency paths to Asia-Pacific
  • Enterprises with high-throughput, latency-sensitive workloads needing resilient backbone routing
  • Businesses requiring multi-cloud integration, direct cloud interconnect, and global reach

name: Dallas Data Center
description:
The Dallas Data Center operated by TeraSwitch is situated within the prominent Equinix InfoMart campus in Dallas, Texas — one of the largest carrier hotels in the United States.

 The facility offers strategic central-US positioning with strong connectivity domestically and internationally (especially as a gateway to Latin America). The site stands out for its high-capacity infrastructure, robust power and cooling architecture, and deep interconnection ecosystem.

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Power & redundancy: The Dallas location features a design capacity of approximately 20 MW of IT-power and operates to a Tier III+ standard with N+1 power design and concurrently maintainable systems
  • Cooling: It uses an N+1 cooling design with A/B dual-powered CRAC units. The published PUE is “1.3 or better” at full load. 
  • Space: The site leverages the InfoMart facility's 500,000+ sq ft of data-centre space.
  • Scalability & flexibility: The data-centre supports rack, cabinet or custom suite deployments, in a carrier-hotel environment enabling fast ramp-up and high-density configurations.
  • Support services: On-site personnel, remote-hands, and full colocation plus connectivity services are available as part of TeraSwitch’s offering.

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • 24/7 security operations with monitoring, badge/biometric access, and controlled access zones (including racks with digital key-code locks and independently locked top/bottom sectors).
  • Fire-protection systems include dual-interlock pre-action dry-pipe sprinklers, comprehensive life-safety systems and environmental monitoring (temperature/humidity/leak detection). 
  • Facility is built within an established carrier-hotel, which implies adherence to industry-standard infrastructure and physical security practices.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Carrier-neutral environment within the InfoMart campus provides access to a large number of network providers, dark-fiber vendors, and internet exchange points (IX). 
  • TeraSwitch’s network infrastructure uses Juniper core switching/routers (QFX, MX series) and offers multi-homed transit, DDoS mitigation, dark fiber and lit-transport services from 100 Mbps to 100 Gbps.
  • Positioned well for both U.S. regional reach and international (Latin America) connectivity owing to Dallas’s strategic role. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises needing central-US colocation with high-density power, strong connectivity and rapid provisioning.
  • Network, content and cloud providers requiring carrier-hotel presence, dark-fiber access and rich interconnection.
  • SaaS, IoT, AI workloads or hybrid-IT deployments that need resilient infrastructure and multi-carrier networking.

name: SDC Intergate Ashburn Building B
description:
Sabey Data Centers’ Intergate.Ashburn Building B is a two-story, mission-critical data center facility located in Ashburn, Loudoun County, Virginia—part of the “Data Center Alley,” one of the densest connectivity corridors in the U.S. 

Operated by Sabey Data Centers, Building B is fully operational (since roughly 2018-2019), with a concrete pre-cast structure offering robust infrastructure for enterprise, colocation, and high-availability deployments.

Its strategic significance comes from its power capacity, proximity to Washington, D.C., and access to multiple fiber providers and carriers, making it an attractive location for companies needing reliable, high-connectivity facilities on the U.S. East Coast. 

⚙️ Facility Highlights

  • Building B delivers 22 MW of mission-critical power, distributed across its data halls.
  • Approximately 160,000 square feet of data hall space configured over two floors. 
  • Structured with a pre-cast concrete shell for durability and scalability. 
  • Modular design: supports flexible build-outs, private data halls or powered shell spaces, with each hall supported by redundant mechanical systems. 
  • Cooling: Building B uses chilled-water systems with CRAH (Computer Room Air Handler) units. 
  • Generator capacity: Building B has on-site generators sized at 2.25 MW with ~48 hour run-time at peak load. 

🔐 Security & Compliance

  • Physical security includes perimeter fencing, secure access checkpoints, CCTV surveillance, and on-site security staff. 
  • The facility maintains redundant electrical and mechanical systems to reduce single points of failure. 
  • Utility provider is Dominion Energy. 
  • Compliance & certifications at the SDC Ashburn campus (which includes Building B) include: HIPAA, HITECH, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, SOC 1 Type 2, SOC 2 Type 2, and SSAE-18. 

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

  • Building B is carrier-neutral. 
  • Multiple Tier-1 and major carriers are present: among them Cogent, CrownCastle, Fiberlight, PacketFabric (SDN), and Zayo (lit and dark fiber). 
  • Redundant fiber entry paths to ensure high availability and minimal risk of single-point fiber failure.
  • Strong interconnection options and cloud-on-ramps; the campus is well-positioned for low-latency connections across the East Coast due to its Ashburn location. 

💼 Who It Serves

  • Enterprises requiring high uptime, strong power availability, and flexible cooling (e.g. high-density compute workloads).
  • Cloud service providers and platforms needing strong interconnection and scalable infrastructure.
  • Financial services, healthcare, or other compliance-sensitive industries, given the facility’s certifications.
  • Organizations needing proximity to federal or government networks (given closeness to Washington, D.C.).
  • Companies looking for modular or powered shell colocation solutions, rather than fully custom build-outs.

name: Anaheim Data Center
description:
Aecero Critical Business Exchange in Anaheim, California provides carrier-neutral colocation and network services from a 110,000 sq ft Orange County data center at 2463 W. La Palma Avenue. The facility supports enterprise colocation, private cabinets, private cages, private suites, network connectivity, cloud interconnect access, and support services for mission-critical infrastructure teams operating in Southern California. Its Anaheim position places it in the Orange County market with access to dense regional fiber routes and Los Angeles interconnection hubs.

The facility is built with 2N electrical infrastructure, eight PowerWare UPS systems, Cyberex digital static transfer switches, four Caterpillar 2,500 kVA generators in an N+1 configuration, a 16,000-gallon fuel reservoir, and a 2,000 kW on-site load bank for testing. Commercial power is delivered by Anaheim Public Utilities through a 12 kV distribution ring service. Cooling infrastructure includes Liebert System3 precision cooling, four EvapCo UBW stainless cooling towers, redundant CRAC units, and a 22,000-gallon makeup water reservoir. Fire protection includes FM-200 clean agent suppression, VESDA detection, and dry-pipe double-interlock preaction sprinklers.

Aecero CBX sits on a major Orange County fiber aggregation point and operates as a carrier-neutral facility with diverse fiber paths into meet-me rooms. Carrier access includes AT&T, Cogent, Cox, Edison, TelePacific, Time Warner Cable, TW Telecom, Verizon, Windstream, XO, Zayo, Wilcon, Level 3, and Global Crossing. Aecero also offers blended internet bandwidth, managed network services, Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute, Google Cloud Interconnect, and dark fiber capacity to downtown Los Angeles carrier hubs at 600 W. 7th Street and One Wilshire.

🏢 Facility Highlights

  1. Building Size: 110,000 sq ft data center facility.
  2. Colocation Options: Half cabinets, full cabinets, private cages, and private suites up to 11,000 sq ft.
  3. Electrical Design: 2N electrical infrastructure with eight PowerWare UPS systems.
  4. Backup Power: Four Caterpillar 2,500 kVA generators with N+1 generator redundancy.
  5. Cooling: Liebert System3 precision cooling and four EvapCo UBW stainless cooling towers.
  6. Fire Protection: FM-200, VESDA, and dry-pipe double-interlock preaction sprinklers.

🔒 Security & Compliance

  1. Security Staffing: On-site security 24x7x365.
  2. Access Control: Biometric two-factor access controls for critical areas.
  3. Surveillance: Digital video surveillance inside and outside the facility.
  4. Client Areas: Individually secured colocation spaces.
  5. Compliance: Annual SSAE16 compliance maintained.

🌐 Connectivity & Carrier Access

name: Grand Prairie Data Center
description:
Provident Data Centers Grand Prairie Data Center Campus in Grand Prairie, Texas is a planned hyperscale development in the Dallas–Fort Worth Metroplex. The campus is designed for large-scale cloud, AI, HPC, and hyperscale infrastructure users that need powered land, build-to-suit capacity, and multi-phase expansion in North Texas. Provident lists the project at 810 acres, 8.5 million developable square feet, 26 planned two-story buildings, and 1.8 GW of total power.

The Grand Prairie campus is under active design and engineering, with the site fully rezoned and permitted. Provident’s current campus page lists Phase 1 delivery for Q1 2027, while the project announcement states the first building is scheduled to be energized in May 2026. The development is structured across multiple phases, with Phase 1 and Phase 2 each planned for 10 buildings and later phases planned for four additional buildings.

The campus is being developed by Provident Data Centers with PowerHouse Data Centers. The announced joint development includes a 1.8 GW switchyard, with the first 500 MW tranche approved through ERCOT. The site is positioned between Dallas and Fort Worth, just outside Grand Prairie in northern Ellis County, giving hyperscale users access to one of the country’s most active cloud, AI, logistics, and enterprise infrastructure markets.

🏢 Facility Highlights

  1. Campus Size: 810 acres.
  2. Developable Area: 8.5 million developable sq ft.
  3. Planned Buildings: 26 total two-story buildings.
  4. Power Capacity: 1.8 GW.
  5. Phase 1: 10 buildings.
  6. Phase 2: 10 buildings.
  7. Later Phases: 4 buildings listed across Phases 3 and 4.
  8. Development Status: Fully rezoned, permitted, and under active design and engineering.

🧠 AI Infrastructure

  1. Target Workloads: Hyperscale cloud, AI, and HPC deployments.
  2. Power Scale: 1.8 GW campus plan with a 500 MW ERCOT-approved first tranche.
  3. Build-To-Suit Model: Designed for large users requiring powered land and scalable campus capacity.
  4. High-Density Planning: Provident positions the campus for next-generation AI and high-performance computing requirements.

🛰️ Network Position

  1. Metroplex Location: Positioned between Dallas and Fort Worth.
  2. Regional Market: Serves the Dallas–Fort Worth data center market.
  3. County Position: Located just outside Grand Prairie in northern Ellis County.
  4. Airport Access: Near Dallas Fort Worth International Airport.

🏗️ Recent Expansions

  1. Announcement: Provident and PowerHouse announced the Grand Prairie hyperscale campus in February 2025.
  2. Power Milestone: First 500 MW tranche approved through ERCOT.
  3. First Energization: First building scheduled for May 2026 energization.
  4. Phase 1 Delivery: Provident’s campus page lists Q1 2027 delivery for Phase 1.

name: Gaffney Data Center
description:
Cielo Digital Infrastructure Gaffney Data Center in Gaffney, South Carolina is a planned hyperscale data center campus at 000 Ford Road in Cherokee County. The project represents Cielo’s first South Carolina development and brings a large-scale digital infrastructure site to the Upstate region between Spartanburg and Charlotte. The campus is planned for cloud, AI, enterprise, and hyperscale infrastructure users requiring large power availability, campus-scale buildings, and energy-focused development.

The Gaffney campus is planned with 300 MW of total power capacity at full build-out and four data center facilities of approximately 400,000 sq ft each, creating an aggregate planned building footprint of 1.6 million sq ft. The development also includes an associated electrical substation. Cielo’s platform focuses on identifying sites with energy access, resilience, and connectivity suited for data center development, positioning the Gaffney campus for high-density compute and large-scale digital infrastructure deployments.

The site’s Cherokee County location gives the project access to South Carolina’s Upstate industrial corridor, the I-85regional spine, the North Carolina state line, Spartanburg, Charlotte, and broader Southeast data center demand. The project is scheduled for initial operations by year-end 2028, giving the campus a phased development path for large-scale customer deployments. Cielo’s Gaffney project is also one of the largest announced technology infrastructure investments in Cherokee County, reinforcing the region’s growing role in AI-ready and power-intensive digital infrastructure.

name: RENO2 Data Center
description:
CENTRA Digital RNO2 Data Center in Reno, Nevada is a planned carrier-neutral interconnection facility at 265 Keystone Ave designed for low-latency connectivity, AI-ready infrastructure, and high-density colocation. RNO2 expands CENTRA’s Reno platform beyond its downtown RNO1 interconnection site and is positioned as Reno’s first purpose-built interconnection data center. The facility is under construction and scheduled for 2026 delivery.

RNO2 is designed with 12 MVA utility power, 8.8 MW of critical capacity, a 230,000 sq ft building footprint, true 2N power, N+1 cooling, air-cooled chillers, four conduit entrances, two meet-me rooms, and more than 15 fiber carriers. The facility is liquid-cooling enabled and supports high-density cabinets up to 150 kW per rack. CENTRA lists a Tier III+ design, PUE of 1.35 or better, SOC 2 Type 2 compliance, and ISO 27001 certification for the RNO2 platform.

The Keystone Avenue location places RNO2 in Reno’s urban connectivity corridor, tethered to CENTRA’s RNO1 meet-me-room site at 200 S. Virginia Street. Reno sits between the San Francisco Bay Area, Silicon Valley, Las Vegas, Salt Lake City, and Northern Nevada’s major industrial data center developments. That network position supports enterprises, carriers, cloud platforms, AI infrastructure teams, and service providers that need interconnection-rich infrastructure closer to Western U.S. traffic routes.

Its location gives AWS additional capacity in a market that supports cloud regions, enterprise platforms, AI workloads, government-adjacent demand, and low-latency connectivity across the Washington, D.C. metropolitan area.
